title: EPIC: Production certificate harness - mathematical invariants as runtime checks
description:
WHY (background): The JAX demos validate their mathematical claims in-process (orthogonality checks in matrix_exponential_gauge_learning.py, 1-Lipschitz margin certificates in tropical demo, bit-exact reversibility ledger in reversible demo, mass conservation in simplicial demo, YBE checks in braid demo). But the PyTorch nanochat ports - the implementations that actually get trained and benchmarked - carry almost NONE of that validation. The theory-to-production bridge is the project's core promise (README "Dual Implementation Strategy"); today the production half of the bridge is unverified.

WHAT (scope, see child beads):
1. `mgr certify`: a CLI command checking each mechanism's DEFINING mathematical invariant directly against the production torch modules, with explicit dtype-aware tolerances, rich output, and JSON artifacts.
2. Property-based tests (Hypothesis) for the algebraic kernels (quaternion/octonion products, tropical semiring, LCP ultrametricity, rotation orthogonality).
3. CI integration + regression-gate wiring so a certificate flipping green-to-red between snapshots fails the gate.
4. Numerical robustness fuzzing across dtypes (bf16/fp32), extreme scales, and adversarial patterns (ties, -inf masking, zero-norm quaternions).

STRATEGIC ROLE: (a) makes the project's claims falsifiable at production scale; (b) acts as the safety net for the EP-A refactor - certificates must pass before and after every migration step; (c) per-release certificate artifacts make the repo credible to outside researchers in a way READMEs cannot.

DELIBERATE ORDERING: this epic's B1 lands BEFORE EP-A's A1 refactor. Building the verification layer first means the refactor is performed under instrumentation instead of on faith.

title: mgr certify: per-mechanism mathematical invariant checks for nanochat
description:
WHY: the JAX demos verify their own math; the torch ports that actually get trained do not. This command IS the theory->production bridge, and it deliberately lands BEFORE the A1 refactor as its safety net.

WHAT: one CLI command (extend cli.py IN PLACE) that instantiates each mechanism at a small config and verifies its defining invariants with explicit dtype-aware tolerances. Rich table (green/yellow/red, measured value vs tolerance) + JSON artifact under artifacts/certs/nanochat/<run_id>/ (same layout conventions as bench-fixed-flops). Exit nonzero on any red.

THE INVARIANT LIST (this is the spec):
- ALL mechanisms - CAUSALITY: gradient of output[t] w.r.t. input[t+k] is exactly 0 (autograd jacobian on tiny shapes, T<=8; the single most important shared certificate).
- standard: softmax row-stochasticity; RoPE norm preservation per 2D pair.
- tropical: 1-Lipschitz in sup-norm (perturb input by eps, output change <= eps); gauge-fix invariance (tropical_gauge_fix shift does not change argmax routes or outputs); reported gamma margins match brute-force runner-up gaps.
- quaternion: rotor-gate norm preservation (unit-q application preserves value norms within tol); qmul associativity and |q1q2|=|q1||q2| on random unit quaternions.
- octonion: norm multiplicativity |xy|=|x||y|; alternativity x(xy)=(xx)y; AND a non-associativity witness - assert the associator [x,y,z] is nonzero for generic triples (guards against silently implementing an associative shortcut that would invalidate the framework's premise).
- reversible: forward->inverse round-trip error ||x - inv(fwd(x))|| <= tol per block (fp32); custom-autograd gradient correctness vs naive autograd on small shapes in fp64.
- gauge: rotation matrices orthogonal (R^T R ~ I); GAUGE INVARIANCE - attention output invariant under a global phase shift of input angles (this is the mechanism's actual claim; test it directly).
- ultrametric: strong triangle inequality structure for hard digits - sim(x,z) >= min(sim(x,y), sim(y,z)); trie-vs-kernel decode parity (already tested in test_demos.py - invoke/reference, do not duplicate).
- braid: YBE law holds when braid_crossing_law=ybe (surface the existing static check's result into the cert artifact); payload multiset invariance in discrete mode.
- simplicial: row-stochastic attention => mass conservation through the y1/y2 paths within tol.
- surreal: SurrealLayer decomposition - direction norm == 1; scaling equivariance (scale input by c, output scales as the linear map predicts).

CLI: mgr certify [--mechanism X] [--device cpu|cuda] [--dtype fp32|bf16] [--seed N] [--artifacts-dir D] [--run-id R].

ACCEPTANCE:
- Clean green run on CPU fp32 for all mechanisms in < 2 minutes.
- Failure path proven: a unit test monkeypatches a mechanism to violate one invariant and asserts certify reports red + nonzero exit.
- JSON schema documented (consumed by B3 CI gate and G2 verdict engine).

PITFALLS: tolerances must be dtype-aware (bf16 needs looser bounds - B4 owns the systematic dtype sweep); keep shapes tiny so the suite stays fast enough to run after every A1 migration step.
notes:
POLISH ADDENDUM (tests & logging):
- VERBOSE MODE: --verbose prints per-check measured value, tolerance, margin-to-tolerance, and duration; run header panel logs seed/device/dtype/config so any red is reproducible from the log alone.
- JSON: include per-check duration_ms and measured values (not just pass/fail) - B3's drift detection and G2's evidence parsing need the numbers.
- LOGGING ON FAILURE: a red check logs the exact tensor stats (shape, dtype, seed) and a one-line repro command (mgr certify --mechanism X --seed N).
- E2E: this command is stage 4 of the rz8.8 pipeline script (fast subset) - keep the fast subset under 2 min CPU permanently; add a runtime regression assertion to its own tests.

title: Property-based tests (Hypothesis) for algebraic kernels
description:
WHY: the B1 harness checks invariants on sampled instances; property-based testing checks the underlying algebraic LAWS over a generated input space and finds the corner cases humans do not write. The algebra implementations (qmul/qconj in quaternion_attention_torch.py, omul/oconj in octonion_attention_torch.py, tropical max-plus ops, ultrametric LCP digit logic, Givens/Cayley rotations) encode textbook axioms that Hypothesis can attack directly.

WHAT: add hypothesis to the [dev] extra in pyproject.toml; add tests/test_algebraic_properties.py (a new TEST file is consistent with the existing tests/ layout and AGENTS.md - genuinely new functionality). Laws to encode:
- Tropical semiring: associativity + commutativity of max (exact on floats - use exact equality), distributivity of + over max, identity element -inf behavior, idempotence max(a,a)=a.
- Quaternions: associativity (with magnitude-scaled fp tolerance), norm multiplicativity, conjugation anti-homomorphism (q1q2)* = q2* q1*.
- Octonions: alternativity x(xy)=(xx)y, Moufang identity, norm multiplicativity, and EXISTENCE of non-associativity (find/assert associator != 0 for generated generic triples).
- Ultrametric LCP on hard digit sequences: d(x,z) <= max(d(x,y), d(y,z)) - the strong triangle inequality, exact on integer digits.
- Rotations from angles (gauge Givens path): orthogonality R^T R = I, det(R) = +1.
- RoPE: per-2D-pair norm preservation.

STRATEGIES: bound float magnitudes (e.g. [-1e3, 1e3]) and scale tolerances with magnitude where laws are only approximately true in fp; use exact equality where the law is exact (tropical max, integer LCP).

ACCEPTANCE:
- Suite runs in CI in < 60s using a registered "ci" Hypothesis profile (max_examples tuned); a "thorough" profile for local deep runs.
- Mutation check performed during development: deliberately break one law (e.g. flip a sign in omul) and confirm Hypothesis catches it; document which seed/example in the close notes.
- Wired into .github/workflows/ci.yml test job (it is just pytest - no workflow change should be needed; verify).

NOTE: independent of A1 - these laws hold before and after the refactor, which is exactly what makes them useful during it.
notes: POLISH ADDENDUM (role change): raised to P1 and wired as a blocker of the A1 AttentionCore refactor (7b0.1). Rationale: during mechanism-by-mechanism migration, instance-level certificates (B1) catch behavioral drift, but the algebra-LAW suite here catches a different class of refactor bug (e.g. an einsum reordering that silently breaks octonion alternativity or tropical distributivity on edge inputs). Both nets must be green after every migration step. Keep the ci profile fast (<60s) so running it per-migration-step stays cheap.

title: Certificates in CI + regression-gate wiring
description:
WHY: certificates only protect the codebase if they run automatically. Two integration points: continuous (every PR) and longitudinal (regression between artifact snapshots).

WHAT:
1. CI: add a fast certificate job to .github/workflows/ci.yml - CPU, fp32, small shapes, deterministic seeds, < 2 min wall-clock (pattern: existing test job sets JAX_PLATFORMS=cpu). Runs `mgr certify` for all mechanisms; red certificate fails the job; keep it a SEPARATE job so a cert failure is immediately distinguishable from a unit-test failure in the PR UI.
2. Regression gate: wire the B1 certificate JSON into the existing `mgr regressions` machinery (cli.py:2279+) so a certificate that flips green->red (or a measured invariant value that degrades past threshold, e.g. reversible round-trip error growing 10x while still under tolerance) between a baseline and candidate snapshot is reported as a regression, honoring --fail-on-regression for CI use.
3. Document the thresholds and the "tightening-only" policy: tolerances may be tightened over time but loosening requires an explicit justification note in the bead/commit.

ACCEPTANCE:
- CI shows the new job; runs green on main.
- A fixture-based test proves the gate trips: synthetic baseline (green) + candidate (red) certificate JSONs -> regressions exits nonzero with a readable report row.
- Certificate values (not just pass/fail) appear in the regression report so slow drift is visible before it crosses tolerance.

DEPENDS ON: B1 (the harness and its JSON schema) and existing bead model_guided_research-ac9 (auto regression gate - coordinate schema and gate plumbing with it rather than building a parallel gate).

title: Numerical robustness fuzzing across dtypes and adversarial inputs
description:
WHY: training uses bf16 mixed precision, but unit tests exercise fp32. The exotic ops have dtype-specific failure modes that fp32 tests never see: cumsum gauge angles drifting in bf16, exp(s) surreal scales overflowing, max-plus arithmetic interacting with -inf masking (NaN from -inf + -inf patterns), p-adic digit rounding near .5 boundaries, qnormalize dividing by ~0 norms. The closed bead model_guided_research-0fw added NaN/Inf watchpoints, but there has been no systematic stress campaign.

WHAT: extend the B1 certify harness (same file/command, fuzz subcommand or --fuzz flag) with a sweep per mechanism:
- dtype in {fp32, bf16} (fp16 optional stretch),
- input scale in {1e-3, 1, 1e3},
- sequence length in {1, 2, 255, 256} (boundary cases around block size),
- adversarial patterns: all-equal inputs (softmax/argmax ties), saturated negative scores (-inf propagation through masking), zero-norm quaternion/octonion inputs (normalization division), alternating extreme +/- values (cancellation).
Record per cell: NaN/Inf occurrence in outputs AND gradients, max abs output, gradient finiteness. Output: mechanism x condition matrix with pass/warn/fail, rich table + JSON artifact.

ACCEPTANCE:
- Full fuzz report artifact committed for the current code state.
- Every FAIL cell either gets a root-cause fix (separate commit referencing this bead) or a filed discovered-from bug bead - finding tracked bugs IS the success condition, not an all-green table.
- Explicit regression coverage for: tie-handling, -inf score rows (a token attending to nothing), zero-norm normalization.

POLICY: do NOT fix failures by silently clamping in the fuzz harness or the mechanism - file beads and fix root causes with documented rationale.

DEPENDS ON: B1 (harness infrastructure).
notes:
POLISH ADDENDUM (tests & logging):
- PROGRESS LOGGING: rich progress bar over the cell matrix (mechanism x dtype x scale x length x pattern); each completed cell logs pass/warn/fail inline.
- FAILURE REPRODUCIBILITY: every FAIL cell logs the generating recipe (mechanism, dtype, scale, T, pattern name, seed) sufficient to reconstruct the failing input WITHOUT rerunning the sweep - the discovered-from bug beads must quote this recipe verbatim.
- REGRESSION CAPTURE: each fixed FAIL becomes a permanent pytest regression case (tiny, deterministic) so fixed bugs stay fixed; the fuzz report links cell -> regression test name once captured.

title: EPIC: Unified attention core, hybrid architectures & performance
description:
WHY (background): The 11 attention mechanisms in nanochat (dispatch in gpt.py Block.__init__, ~gpt.py:326-363; implementations in nanochat/*_torch.py) each independently re-implement the same scaffolding: QKV projection, GQA head repetition (repeat_kv_heads), RoPE, causal masking (model_utils.causal_attn_mask), KV-cache insertion, and the three decode paths (train Tq==Tk / single-token Tq==1 / chunked Tq>1) that CausalSelfAttention (gpt.py:185-305) implements canonically. This ~10x duplication means every cross-cutting fix is an 11-file change, every new framework costs a full re-implementation, and the single most promising research direction named in the README ("Hybrid Architectures": different attention types per layer, ensembles, adaptive routing) is structurally blocked.

WHAT (scope of this epic, see child beads):
1. Factor shared scaffolding into one place while preserving each mechanism's math EXACTLY (the EP-B certificate harness is the safety net and intentionally lands first).
2. Unlock per-layer mechanism schedules and a learned Mixture-of-Mechanisms (MoM) router - a genuinely novel architecture and the project's best shot at a headline result.
3. Close known production gaps: gauge block raises NotImplementedError on KV-cache (gauge_block_torch.py ~line 81); octonion attention runs an O(T^2) per-query Python loop (octonion_attention_torch.py ~line 159).
4. Open the path to custom Triton kernels (tropical max-plus first).

STRATEGIC VALUE: turns the repo from "11 parallel experiments" into "a composable algebra of attention mechanisms". Composability is what makes frameworks #12/#13 (EP-F) cheap to add, the standalone API (EP-H) presentable, and hybrids/scorecards (EP-C) possible.

CONSTRAINTS (AGENTS.md): revise files in place (gpt.py, model_utils.py, existing *_torch.py); NO V2/improved/unified file forks; an incredibly high bar for any new code file. All console output rich-styled.

ORDERING: B1 (certificate harness) must be green BEFORE the A1 refactor begins, and re-run green after each mechanism migration.

title: Refactor: extract shared AttentionCore scaffolding across all 11 mechanisms
description:
WHY: Every mechanism file (tropical_attention_torch.py, ultrametric_attention_torch.py, braid_attention_torch.py, fractal_attention_torch.py, quaternion_attention_torch.py, octonion_attention_torch.py, simplicial_attention_torch.py, surreal_torch.py) duplicates the scaffolding that CausalSelfAttention (gpt.py:185-305) implements canonically: QKV projection, GQA repeat_kv_heads, RoPE application, causal_attn_mask handling, KV-cache insert, and the three decode paths (Tq==Tk train / Tq==1 decode / Tq>1 chunked decode). Historically a fix in one copy does not propagate to the other ten. This duplication blocks per-layer schedules (A2), MoM routing (A3), cheap addition of frameworks #12/#13 (EP-F), and a presentable standalone API (H1).

WHAT: Introduce a single scaffold (revise gpt.py and/or model_utils.py IN PLACE per AGENTS.md - no new file unless genuinely unavoidable) where a mechanism contributes only its distinguishing operations: score(q,k)->affinities and aggregate(affinities,v)->y, plus optional pre/post hooks for mechanisms that need them (gauge transport, braid permutation accumulation, ultrametric digit projections to_digits_q/to_digits_k). Mechanism modules keep their files but shrink to the math.

HOW (migration discipline):
1. Design the protocol off the union of current signatures; document it with docstrings in code.
2. Migrate standard attention first with a golden test proving zero behavior change.
3. Migrate one mechanism at a time; after EACH migration run `mgr certify` (B1) and the tests/test_demos.py KV-cache parity tests. Never migrate two mechanisms in one commit.
4. Preserve ALL config flags (tropical_*, ultrametric_*, braid_*) and buffer-stat logging (e.g. tropical_gamma_head_mean/min) byte-for-byte in summary outputs.

BOUNDARY DECISIONS (document in code): reversible (halves channels, wraps attention as F, requires even n_head) and gauge (replaces the whole block incl. MLP) are BLOCK-level specializations - do not force them into the core protocol; define and document where the scaffold's responsibility ends.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- All existing tests pass; B1 certificate suite green before AND after with identical or tighter tolerances.
- Fixed-seed 50-step CPU training run per mechanism: loss trajectory identical to pre-refactor (bitwise where dtype allows, else atol<=1e-6) - capture pre-refactor goldens FIRST.
- Net LOC reduction in mechanism files; no public flag or CLI change.

PITFALLS: torch.compile interactions (keep dispatch static per layer, no data-dependent Python branching in hot path); do not break the WeakKeyDictionary trie cache in ultrametric; keep buffer registration names stable (external tooling reads them).
notes:
POLISH ADDENDUM (migration discipline + logging):
- The per-migration verification gate is now THREE nets, all green after EACH mechanism migration: (1) mgr certify (B1/5ki.1), (2) the Hypothesis algebra-law suite (B2/5ki.2 - now a wired dependency), (3) tests/test_demos.py KV-cache parity tests.
- GOLDEN-CAPTURE LOGGING: when capturing pre-refactor goldens (fixed-seed 50-step loss trajectories per mechanism), store them as committed fixture files with a manifest (mechanism, seed, config hash, torch version) and log the capture conditions - a golden that cannot be re-derived is not a golden.
- MIGRATION LOG: maintain a short per-mechanism migration checklist in the PR/commit messages (mechanism, nets run, golden match result, LOC before/after) so the 11-step campaign is auditable afterward.

title: Per-layer attention schedules (heterogeneous mechanism stacks)
description:
WHY: README "Future Directions -> Hybrid Architectures". Theory assigns different comparative advantages per mechanism (gauge: stability; ultrametric: hierarchy; tropical: robustness; standard: general). Layers plausibly want different inductive biases (e.g. gauge early for stable feature extraction, ultrametric mid for hierarchical routing, standard late for generic mixing). Today GPTConfig.attention_type is one global string - heterogeneous stacks are impossible without code edits.

WHAT: Allow GPTConfig.attention_type to accept a per-layer list. CLI: --attention-schedule "gauge,ultrametric,standard,standard" (length must equal n_layer, or be a shorter repeating pattern - define and document the expansion rule). Validate per-mechanism constraints PER LAYER at config time with actionable error messages: head_dim%4 (quaternion), head_dim%8 (octonion), n_embd even (gauge/reversible), n_head even (reversible).

KV-CACHE: engine.KVCache must support per-layer geometry differences; simplicial y1 cache lanes only for simplicial layers (precedent: engine.py ensure_simplicial_y1_cache); schedule validation REJECTS gauge in cached-generation paths until A5 lands (clear error naming the bead).

ARTIFACTS: summary.json records the full resolved schedule; bench-fixed-flops accepts schedule strings as variants; estimate_flops() (gpt.py:610) currently assumes homogeneous layers - extend to sum per-layer estimates.

OPTIMIZER: verify Muon's matrix-parameter grouping (setup_optimizers, gpt.py:623-664) remains correct for heterogeneous blocks (different blocks expose different parameter shapes).

ACCEPTANCE:
- Mixed-schedule training runs on CPU smoke config; generation works for cache-supporting mixes.
- Tests: config validation errors (wrong length, invalid mechanism, constraint violation per layer), per-layer dispatch correctness (each layer instantiates the intended class), KV parity test for a mixed stack (full forward vs incremental decode).
- run.md/summary.json show the schedule; regressions tooling treats schedule string as the variant key.

DEPENDS ON: A1 (shared scaffolding makes per-layer instantiation uniform).
notes:
POLISH ADDENDUM (tests & logging):
- STARTUP LOGGING: at model build, log the fully-resolved per-layer schedule as a rich table (layer index, mechanism, per-mechanism constraint check result) so a misconfigured schedule is visible before any compute is spent.
- E2E TEST: tiny CPU training run with a mixed schedule asserting (a) summary.json records the resolved schedule, (b) each Block instantiated the intended class (introspect model.blocks), (c) metrics.jsonl present and parseable.
- NEGATIVE TESTS with message-quality assertions: wrong schedule length, unknown mechanism name, per-layer constraint violation (e.g. octonion with head_dim%8!=0 at layer 2) - each error must name the offending LAYER INDEX and the fix.
- KV parity test for a mixed stack must cover both single-token and chunked decode paths.

title: Mixture-of-Mechanisms (MoM): learned routing across attention mechanisms
description:
WHY: the most ambitious architectural idea available to this project: instead of choosing a mathematical structure per run (or per layer, A2), let the model LEARN which structure to apply. This converts the 11-way A/B question into differentiable architecture search, and the learned routing weights are publishable evidence by themselves ("which math does a transformer prefer, per depth and per token type?").

WHAT: a MoM block that runs K member mechanisms in parallel and blends outputs y = sum_i g_i(x) * y_i with a learned gate. Start with the cheap, KV-cache-compatible subset: standard, tropical, ultrametric(kernel), quaternion, fractal. Three routing modes, in implementation order:
1. per-layer scalar gates (softmax over K learned logits),
2. per-token gating: gate = softmax(Linear(norm(x))) applied tokenwise,
3. straight-through top-1 hard routing to recover compute (optional stretch).
CLI: --attention-type mom --mom-mechanisms standard,tropical,... --mom-routing layer|token|top1 --mom-balance-coef <float> (MoE-style load-balancing aux loss to prevent router collapse).

DIAGNOSTICS: log routing entropy and per-mechanism utilization as registered buffers (pattern: tropical_gamma_* in tropical_attention_torch.py) and emit them into the D2 metrics stream.

KV-CACHE: each member keeps its own cache lane => memory cost ~K x; document honestly and default-cap K<=4.

HONEST ACCOUNTING: compute cost is ~K x per layer. Fixed-FLOPs comparisons must use measured FLOPs/wall-clock, not the homogeneous analytic estimate (relates existing bead model_guided_research-bks "FLOPs vs measured TFLOPs validation").

ACCEPTANCE:
- Trains stably on CPU smoke config; routing weights visible in metrics JSONL.
- Unit tests: gate gradient flow (router weights move from init on seeded data); non-collapse with balance loss (utilization entropy above threshold after N steps); KV parity in layer-gating mode; config validation (unknown member, K too large).
- A4 covers the benchmark; this bead only needs a smoke-scale sanity comparison vs standard.

DEPENDS ON: A2 (per-layer machinery and heterogeneous KV-cache are prerequisites).
notes:
POLISH ADDENDUM (tests & logging):
- E2E SMOKE: short CPU training with --attention-type mom asserting router stats (per-member utilization, routing entropy) appear in metrics.jsonl (D2) at every log_interval and are non-degenerate by run end.
- LIVE LOGGING: per-layer member utilization logged at log_interval (compact rich line); COLLAPSE WARNING: if utilization entropy stays below threshold for K consecutive intervals, emit a loud rich warning suggesting --mom-balance-coef increase - silent router collapse is this architecture's primary failure mode.
- UNIT TESTS: gate gradient flow; balance-loss effect (entropy with vs without aux loss on seeded data); straight-through estimator correctness for top1 mode (forward uses argmax, backward uses soft grads); KV parity in layer-gating mode; cache memory accounting matches K-lane expectation.

title: Hybrid-vs-monolith benchmark at fixed FLOPs
description:
WHY: claims about hybrid stacks are worthless without the same fixed-budget, multi-seed, CI-bearing harness used for monoliths. This bead is the empirical payoff of A2/A3.

WHAT: extend `mgr bench-fixed-flops` (cli.py:1142+) to accept schedule strings and MoM variants, then run a curated comparison:
(a) all-standard baseline;
(b) three hand-designed schedules, each with its theoretical motivation documented inline in the report:
    - "stability->hierarchy->generic": gauge, ultrametric, standard, standard
    - "robust front-end": tropical, tropical, standard, standard
    - "geometry mid-stack": standard, quaternion, quaternion, standard
(c) MoM-layer with K=3 (standard/tropical/ultrametric) if A3 has landed;
>=3 seeds each; report mean +/- CI for final loss, tokens/s, peak memory.

OUTPUT: suite report.md + summary.json under artifacts/bench/hybrids/<run_id>/ using the existing artifact layout; schema compatible with the `mgr regressions` command so hybrid runs participate in regression gating. Statistical comparison reuses the Mann-Whitney U / effect-size machinery already present in cli.py (per-head-metrics command).

ACCEPTANCE:
- Report ranks variants with effect sizes and CIs; one explicit verdict line per theory motivation (supported/refuted at this scale).
- Runs reproducible from a single documented command; seeds and configs recorded in summary.json.

RELATED: existing bead model_guided_research-z7r (granular A/B feature benchmarking) shares harness patterns - coordinate plumbing, do not duplicate; this bead specifically answers the hybrid question. MoM FLOPs accounting caveat from A3 applies (use measured wall-clock alongside analytic budget).

DEPENDS ON: A2 (schedules). A3 optional - run (c) only if available; do not block on it.

title: Gauge block: KV-cache + GQA support (remove NotImplementedError)
description:
WHY: gauge_block_torch.py raises NotImplementedError when a kv_cache is passed (~line 81) and requires n_kv_head==n_head. It is the ONLY mechanism excluded from generation, which blocks `mgr sample` full coverage (D5), gauge participation in cached hybrid schedules (A2), and any future serving story. tests/test_demos.py currently asserts the rejection - that test flips to a parity test when this lands.

TECHNICAL CORE: the obstacle is the cumulative gauge field - global angles are a cumsum of per-token local angles, so token t's frame depends on all previous tokens. SOLUTION: cache the running cumulative angle (a per-stream (B, d/2) fp32 tensor) in the KVCache as a per-layer extra-state lane, following the simplicial y1 precedent (engine.py ensure_simplicial_y1_cache / insert_simplicial_y1). At decode step t: global_angle_t = cached_cumsum + local_angle(x_t); transport x_t into the global frame; attend against cached keys ALREADY STORED IN THE GLOBAL FRAME (transport is applied per-token at insert time, so cached keys remain valid - write the invariance argument as a code comment, it is the correctness crux).

ALSO: lift the n_kv_head==n_head restriction by applying repeat_kv_heads after transport, matching the standard path.

NUMERICS: angle accumulation in bf16 drifts over long sequences - keep the cumsum buffer fp32 regardless of autocast, and document this.

ACCEPTANCE:
- Parity test mirroring the tests/test_demos.py KV pattern: full-sequence forward vs token-by-token incremental decode, and vs chunked decode, atol per dtype.
- Long-decode drift test: 2k-step decode, max deviation from full-forward bounded and recorded.
- A2's schedule validation updated to allow gauge in cached paths; D5 sampling includes gauge.
- B1 gauge certificates (orthogonality, gauge invariance) pass in cached mode too.

DEPENDS ON: A1 (land after the refactor so cache plumbing is written once against the final scaffold; gauge stays a block-level specialization per A1's boundary decision).
notes:
POLISH ADDENDUM (tests & logging):
- DEBUG LOGGING: optional flag (e.g. --gauge-cache-debug / env) logging per-decode-step cache state: position, fp32 cumsum norm, drift estimate vs full-forward reference on the first N steps - makes drift bugs diagnosable from logs alone.
- E2E: once landed, gauge must join the mgr sample determinism scenario (rz8.8 Scenario 3) and the mixed-schedule generation test in A2; update the test that currently ASSERTS NotImplementedError to assert parity instead (do not leave a stale expected-failure).
- UNIT TESTS: cumsum-lane save/restore through D1 checkpointing (a resumed generation must continue the gauge field exactly); GQA path (n_kv_head < n_head) parity after the restriction is lifted.

title: Octonion attention: vectorize the per-query Python loop
description:
WHY: octonion_attention_torch.py (~line 159) loops over Tq in Python computing (Q * K_conj) * V per query to avoid materializing a (B,H,Tq,Tk,N,8) tensor. This makes octonion training orders of magnitude slower than peers and poisons every fixed-FLOPs/wall-clock comparison it appears in (budget spent in Python overhead, not math). It also makes octonion infeasible for the EP-E scaling ladder.

WHAT: replace the loop with CHUNKED vectorization: process Tq in tiles of size c, computing the non-associative product with batched einsum over the tile. Choose c automatically from a memory budget: B*H*c*Tk*N*8*dtype_size <= budget (expose --octonion-tile-budget or derive from available memory; document default). CRITICAL: octonion multiplication is NON-ASSOCIATIVE - the exact parenthesization ((Q*K_conj)*V) must be preserved; any "optimization" that reorders the product changes the mathematics.

ACCEPTANCE:
- Outputs match the current loop implementation on seeded inputs to atol<=1e-6 fp32 (target bitwise where reduction order is unchanged) across shapes: Tq in {1, 7, 256}, GQA on/off, B in {1, 4}.
- >=5x wall-clock improvement on CPU at T=256 (record before/after numbers in the close notes; store microbench JSON under artifacts/perf/ following the pattern of existing bead model_guided_research-2cg per-op microbench).
- Property test added to the B2 suite asserting alternativity x(xy)=(xx)y and norm multiplicativity |xy|=|x||y| hold identically for the vectorized path (guards against silent reordering).
- Decode path (Tq==1) remains fast - no regression.

PITFALLS: omul builds octonions from 2x2 quaternion blocks via Cayley-Dickson (o1*o2 = (ac - d_conj*b, d*a + b*c_conj)) - keep that structure; einsum over blades must reproduce the same multiplication table (verify against a brute-force table test).

DEPENDS ON: A1 (vectorize once, against the final scaffold interface).
notes:
POLISH ADDENDUM (tests & logging):
- INIT LOGGING: log the auto-chosen tile size and the estimated peak intermediate memory once at module init (debug level) - the tile heuristic must be auditable from logs.
- E2E MICROBENCH SCRIPT: before/after wall-clock + peak-mem JSON artifact under artifacts/perf/ including env info (device, dtype, torch version); rich comparison table printed; this artifact is the bead's evidence of the >=5x claim.
- UNIT TESTS: tile-boundary correctness (Tq not divisible by tile size; Tq smaller than one tile; Tq==1 decode), brute-force multiplication-table equivalence test for the einsum path, OOM-guard behavior when the budget forces tile=1 (must degrade to current per-query behavior, not crash).

title: Tropical max-plus Triton kernel (first custom kernel)
description:
WHY: README Open Question #4: "Hardware: custom kernels for exotic operations?" Tropical attention is the best first target: max-plus GEMM (tropical_max_plus_attention, tropical_attention_torch.py:19-85) is structurally identical to matmul with (+,*) replaced by (max,+), so a Triton tile kernel is a direct transcription of the classic matmul tiling - and tropical is among the most promising mechanisms (1-Lipschitz robustness certificates). The current implementation is memory-bound through broadcast intermediates.

WHAT:
1. Forward Triton kernel: score(q,k) = max_d(q_d + k_d) fused with causal masking, then y_d = max_k(score + v_kd).
2. Custom backward: the subgradient routes through argmax indices - store argmax_d (scores) and argmax_k (aggregation) per output during forward (this mirrors exactly how route extraction works in the JAX demo tropical_geometry_and_idempotent_algebra.py; the kernel byproduct gives us per-sample discrete routes at production scale for free - note this for interpretability follow-ups).
3. Integration behind --tropical-kernel triton|reference with automatic fallback when Triton/CUDA is unavailable (CPU CI must pass untouched).
4. Margin statistics (tropical_gamma_*) computed as a kernel byproduct (runner-up tracking) so the diagnostics don't disappear in fast mode.

TIES POLICY: multiple argmax => deterministic first-index selection in BOTH kernel and reference; assert agreement in tests (otherwise gradients differ silently).

ACCEPTANCE:
- Parity vs reference: max/+ are exact ops, so same-dtype outputs must match bitwise on tie-free inputs; tie-handling test passes; gradcheck (fp64 reference path) on small shapes.
- Speedup >=3x at T>=1024 on GPU, recorded in artifacts/perf/.
- B1 tropical certificates (1-Lipschitz, gauge-fix invariance, margin consistency) pass with the kernel enabled.

DEPENDS ON: A1 (stable integration surface) and existing bead model_guided_research-c6h (Triton hotspot benchmarking plan - confirms tropical is the top-value target and sets measurement methodology BEFORE we invest in kernel work).
notes:
POLISH ADDENDUM (tests & logging):
- PATH LOGGING: log the active kernel path (triton|reference) and the REASON (flag, no CUDA, no triton import) exactly once at model build - benchmark results must never be ambiguous about which path ran; record the path in summary.json.

title: EPIC: Hypothesis ledger & operationalized AI-guided research loop
description:
WHY (background): the project's entire identity is "AI-guided research" - the genesis was GPT-5 Pro generating additional research directions and scoring its own proposals on a 0-1000 rubric (README sections "Genesis" and "The AI Self-Evaluation Framework"). Yet nothing in the repo operationalizes that loop: predictions live in README prose, verdicts live scattered across artifacts/ directories, and the cycle "AI proposes -> human+AI implement -> results update beliefs -> informed next proposals" has no machinery. Repos with benchmarks are common; a repo that PREREGISTERS its claims machine-readably and auto-adjudicates them against artifacts is novel research infrastructure - and it compounds, because every future experiment plugs into it.

WHAT (scope, see child beads):
1. G1: a versioned machine-readable hypothesis registry (statement, source/provenance incl. "model-proposed", prediction with exact metric path + comparator + threshold, status lifecycle open/supported/refuted/inconclusive/blocked, append-only verdict history). Seeded by encoding ALL existing claims from the README and the 11 framework docs (~25-30 entries).
2. G2: an automatic verdict engine (`mgr adjudicate`) that locates qualifying artifacts (summary.json, D2 metrics JSONL, C2 eval-tasks JSON, B1 certify JSON, E2 scaling fits), applies preregistered comparators with a CI-aware statistical policy, appends verdicts (never silently flips), and emits a ledger scoreboard report.
3. G3: the documented research-loop protocol (how AI-proposed hypotheses enter, get scored on the existing rubric, become beads via br with discovered-from links, get adjudicated) so future agents extend the registry rather than the README.

STRATEGIC VALUE: the most differentiating epic - it makes the project's meta-thesis (AI as research partner) executable rather than anecdotal, and it imposes preregistration discipline that makes every EP-C/EP-E result more credible.

title: Hypothesis registry + mgr hypotheses CLI (preregistration infrastructure)
description:
WHY: see EP-G epic rationale. The registry is the load-bearing artifact: claims live in prose today (README "Why It Matters" bullets, the Expected Performance table, the framework docs' predictions); none are machine-checkable, so none ever get formally confirmed or retired.

WHAT:
1. SCHEMA + STORAGE: a versioned registry file (hypotheses/registry.yaml - one new DATA file, not code proliferation; directory keeps future per-study files tidy). Entry schema:
   {id (stable slug), statement (one-sentence prose claim), mechanism(s), source {kind: human|model, provenance: e.g. "GPT-5 Pro initial self-scoring session, 2025" | "README v1 Expected Performance table"}, date_registered, prediction {metric_path (exact path into a named artifact schema: C2 eval JSON / D2 metrics / B1 certify JSON / E2 fits), comparator (>=, <=, delta-vs-baseline), baseline spec (mechanism + equal-FLOPs requirement), threshold/effect size, min_seeds, scale_caveats}, status (open|supported|refuted|inconclusive|blocked), evidence [artifact paths + run_ids], verdict_history [append-only {date, verdict, artifact refs, adjudicator: human|engine-version}]}.
2. SEEDING (the bulk of the work): encode ALL existing claims - the 11 frameworks' README "Why It Matters" bullets, the Expected Performance table, and the falsifiable-predictions sections of the framework docs (~25-30 entries). Each entry traceable to its prose source. Claims too vague to operationalize get status=open with prediction=null and a note "needs operationalization" - visible debt, not silent omission (C3 operationalizes the task-battery subset).
3. CLI: `mgr hypotheses list [--status S] [--mechanism M] / show ID / add (interactive or flags) / validate` - rich tables, --json mode for tooling. Validation: schema-check all entries, verify metric_path points at a registered artifact schema, reject duplicate ids.
4. GOVERNANCE: registry changes flow through normal git review; verdict_history is APPEND-ONLY (the validate command rejects history rewrites by comparing against the committed parent version when available).

IMPLEMENTATION NOTE: prefer stdlib dataclasses + pyyaml-free JSON if avoiding new deps matters - or YAML via a tiny vendored loader decision documented in-code; pydantic only if [server] extra is acceptable to require for the CLI path (decide in implementation, document why).

ACCEPTANCE: registry seeded with all existing claims; `mgr hypotheses validate` green and tested (malformed fixture rejected); README's speculative table REPLACED with a pointer to the registry + a generated summary table (the README stops accumulating untracked claims).

title: Automatic verdict engine (mgr adjudicate)
description:
WHY: a registry nobody adjudicates is a wish list. The engine closes the loop: artifacts in, verdicts out, deterministically and re-runnably - the mechanical half of "AI-guided research with receipts".

WHAT: `mgr adjudicate [--hypothesis ID | --all] [--artifacts DIR...] [--dry-run]`:
1. MATCHING: for each hypothesis, locate qualifying artifacts across the known schemas (summary.json, D2 metrics.jsonl, C2 eval-tasks JSON, B1 certify JSON, E2 scaling fits) by mechanism + metric_path + the prediction's qualification requirements (equal-FLOPs configs, min_seeds, matched data snapshot).
2. GATEKEEPING (garbage-in protection): if qualifying artifacts are missing or fail requirements (seed count too low, budgets mismatched), the verdict is BLOCKED with a machine-readable reason - the engine must REFUSE to adjudicate weakly rather than produce a soft verdict. This rule is the engine's integrity core; test it explicitly.
3. STATISTICAL POLICY: multi-seed mean + CI (bootstrap or t-based, documented); SUPPORTED if the CI clears the threshold in the predicted direction; REFUTED if it clears in the opposite direction; INCONCLUSIVE if the CI straddles. Policy version recorded in each verdict (so a future policy change cannot silently reinterpret history).
4. LEDGER SEMANTICS: append a verdict_history entry (date, verdict, evidence refs, engine version); NEVER mutate prior entries; status field updates to the latest verdict.
5. REPORT: the ledger scoreboard - markdown (and HTML reusing the regressions HTML/CSS machinery in cli.py): one row per hypothesis with status, latest evidence, and verdict history; sorted by status then mechanism. This is the public face of the project's epistemic state.
6. INTEGRATION: invoked as the final step by C4 (scorecard) and E2 (scaling report); also runnable standalone over any artifact tree.

ACCEPTANCE:
- End-to-end test with fixture artifacts driving one hypothesis to EACH verdict state (supported/refuted/inconclusive/blocked);
- determinism: same artifacts -> byte-identical verdicts (fixed bootstrap seed);
- append-only enforcement tested (attempted history rewrite detected by validate);
- first real adjudication run committed: the ledger report for whatever evidence exists at execution time (expect mostly BLOCKED - that snapshot of honest ignorance is itself valuable and should be committed).

DEPENDS ON: G1 (registry + schemas), D2 (metrics stream is the most common evidence source).
notes:
POLISH ADDENDUM (tests & logging):
- EXPLAINED VERDICTS: the adjudication log explains every verdict in plain language: which artifacts were considered, which qualified, which were REJECTED and on exactly which requirement (seeds too few, budget mismatch, schema version unknown) - BLOCKED reasons must be human-actionable next steps, not error codes.
- DRY-RUN: --dry-run prints the full evidence-matching plan (hypothesis -> candidate artifacts -> qualification results) without writing any verdict; this is also the debugging tool for metric_path mistakes in registry entries.
- SCHEMA-VERSION GUARD TEST: an artifact with an unknown schema_version must produce BLOCKED with a clear reason, never a silently-wrong parse.
- MAINTENANCE CONTRACT: add an adjudicate stage (fixture hypothesis driven to SUPPORTED) to the rz8.8 e2e pipeline script as part of this bead's acceptance.
DEPENDENCY NOTE: now also depends on B1 (5ki.1) - certify JSON is a first-class evidence schema this engine must parse; the fixture set must include a certificate-based hypothesis.

title: AI-research-loop protocol documentation + worked example
description:
WHY: the meta-loop (AI proposes -> scores -> human+AI implement -> adjudicate -> beliefs update -> better proposals) is the project's actual thesis (README Genesis + AI Self-Evaluation Framework), but it currently exists only as origin-story prose. Documenting it as an executable protocol means future sessions and agents EXTEND the registry instead of accreting untracked claims in the README - and makes the loop reproducible by anyone.

WHAT: docs/research_loop.md (new doc file in the existing docs/ directory) covering:
1. THE LOOP, step by step: proposal (human- or model-originated) -> scoring on the existing 0-1000 rubric (theoretical novelty / practical feasibility / potential impact / mathematical rigor / implementation clarity - the README's dimensions, captured here as a REUSABLE PROMPT TEMPLATE with the exact wording used for AI scoring sessions) -> registration in the G1 registry with falsification criteria -> bead creation via br with discovered-from links (hypothesis id <-> bead id cross-referencing convention defined here) -> implementation -> G2 adjudication -> registry update -> next-cycle proposals informed by the ledger state.
2. CONVENTIONS: how hypothesis ids map to bead ids and git branches; where scoring transcripts get archived (provenance for model-proposed entries); when a REFUTED hypothesis spawns a revised successor vs gets retired.
3. WORKED EXAMPLE: one hypothesis walked through the complete cycle with real artifacts. Use whatever has actually completed by execution time - the tropical 1-Lipschitz certificate (B1) is the safe default (claim -> registry entry -> certify artifact -> adjudication -> SUPPORTED verdict in the ledger); a C4 scorecard row is the richer option if available.
4. AGENTS.md gains a short pointer section (a few lines, not duplication) so every future agent session discovers the protocol.

ACCEPTANCE: doc complete; the worked example uses REAL committed artifacts (no hypothetical paths); the scoring prompt template is copy-paste runnable; AGENTS.md pointer added.

DEPENDS ON: G1 (registry must exist; the worked example needs it). G2 strengthens the example but a manual adjudication is acceptable if G2 has not landed - note which was used.

Keep the existing crossing_update as the fast restricted mode.

title: EPIC: Frameworks #12-13 - Clifford (geometric algebra) & hyperbolic attention
description:
WHY (background): the project's proven growth pattern is dual implementation: markdown theory doc -> JAX demo with in-process property checks -> nanochat torch attention -> benchmarks. The three existing long-horizon framework epics (model_guided_research-bya category theory, model_guided_research-my1 topos theory, model_guided_research-7dm derived algebra) are deliberately speculative p4 placeholders with no concrete implementation path. This epic adds the two NEAREST-term, highest-confidence framework candidates:

#12 CLIFFORD / GEOMETRIC ALGEBRA: strictly generalizes the quaternion mechanism (Cl(0,2) is isomorphic to the quaternions - giving an exact reduction test) and relates to octonions; graded products (geometric = inner + wedge) give rotation-equivariant features in arbitrary dimension; there is an active literature to position against (Geometric Algebra Transformers, Clifford-group-equivariant networks), which makes the work legible to reviewers; it is the natural algebraic sibling of the gauge block.

#13 HYPERBOLIC ATTENTION: negative-curvature embedding geometry for hierarchies; complements ultrametric (CONTINUOUS hierarchy bias vs p-adic DISCRETE hierarchy bias) and fractal (self-similar addressing), enabling a three-way "which hierarchy bias wins" study on the hierarchical-retrieval diagnostic task (EP-C task 3) that, to our knowledge, no one has run.

WHAT (scope, see child beads): F1/F4 design docs in the house style of markdown_documentation/ (first-principles derivations, complexity analysis, falsifiable predictions sections that feed the EP-G registry); F2/F5 JAX demos with property checks registered in cli.py DEMOS; F3/F6 nanochat attention modules built on the EP-A AttentionCore scaffolding (F3 doubles as the refactor's "is adding a mechanism actually easy now?" acceptance test).

CONVENTIONS: new root demo file + new nanochat/*_attention_torch.py file per framework are justified under AGENTS.md (genuinely new functionality matching the established one-file-per-framework pattern). Predictions must be preregistered (EP-G) BEFORE benchmarking.

title: Clifford/geometric-algebra framework design doc (framework #12)
description:
WHY: see EP-F epic rationale - Clifford algebra is the nearest-term high-confidence framework addition: it strictly generalizes the quaternion mechanism (Cl(0,2) ~ H gives an EXACT reduction test), relates to octonions, has rotation-equivariance payoff in arbitrary dimension, and has an active literature to position against (which makes results legible).

WHAT: write markdown_documentation/clifford_algebra_and_geometric_attention.md in the established house style (the other 11 docs are 24-45KB: first-principles derivation -> connections to literature -> complexity analysis -> falsifiable predictions -> implementation plan):
- MATH: Clifford algebras Cl(p,q); graded structure (scalar/vector/bivector/...); geometric product = inner + wedge; rotors as even-grade elements generalizing quaternions; sandwich product R x R~ for rotations; grade projection operators.
- ALGEBRA CHOICE (analyze both, recommend one): Cl(3,0) - 8-dimensional, fits the head_dim%8 pattern octonion already uses, full 3D geometric semantics; vs Cl(2,0) - 4-dimensional, cheaper, fits head_dim%4. Decision criteria: parameter efficiency, multiplication-table cost, what the diagnostic tasks (C1 task 5) can actually detect.
- MECHANISM DESIGN: multivector-valued features; rotor-gated value aggregation generalizing the quaternion rotor-gate (quaternion_attention_torch.py y = Q*(K_conj*V) is the template). KEY CORRECTNESS ANCHOR: restricted to the even subalgebra of Cl(0,2), the mechanism must reduce EXACTLY to the existing quaternion attention - derive this reduction in an appendix symbolically; it becomes the F2/F3 acceptance test.
- POSITIONING: vs Geometric Algebra Transformer (Brehmer et al.) and Clifford-group-equivariant networks - state precisely what is DIFFERENT here (rotor-gate attention design + certificate-first validation + the scorecard methodology).
- PREREGISTERED PREDICTIONS section (feeds C3/G1): e.g. "clifford beats quaternion on rotation-composition-with-translations by >= 2 points at equal FLOPs"; "norm preservation certificate holds to fp32 tolerance"; "matches quaternion on pure-rotation tasks (reduction sanity)".
- IMPLEMENTATION PLAN detailed enough that F2/F3 require zero further design decisions (basis-blade multiplication table generation, normalization strategy, initialization).

ACCEPTANCE: doc complete in house style; the quaternion-reduction identity verified symbolically (sympy scratch work acceptable, conclusion in the appendix); predictions written in registry-compatible form.

title: Clifford JAX demo with property checks (12th demo)
description:
WHY: house pipeline step 2: every framework gets a self-contained JAX demo with in-process property validation before production implementation. The demo is where the multiplication table and rotor machinery get debugged cheaply.

WHAT: new root-level demo module (new file justified: genuinely new framework, matching the established one-file-per-framework pattern), e.g. clifford_algebra_and_geometric_attention.py, registered as the 12th entry in cli.py DEMOS dict, with demo() entry point and __main__ guard per house pattern:
- Geometric product for the F1-chosen algebra with the basis-blade multiplication table BUILT PROGRAMMATICALLY (signs from blade reordering/contraction rules), not hand-typed - then verified against known identities (e1e2e1 = -e2 in Cl(3,0), etc.).
- PROPERTY CHECKS in-demo (house style - the demo prints a rich property-check table):
  1. quaternion-subalgebra reduction: embed random quaternions into the even subalgebra, geometric product must match qmul EXACTLY (the F1 appendix identity, now numerical);
  2. rotor norm preservation: R x R~ preserves vector norms for unit rotors;
  3. grade-projection orthogonality and completeness (sum of grade projections = identity);
  4. associativity of the geometric product (unlike octonions! - this contrast with the octonion demo is pedagogically valuable, say it in the output);
  5. rotor composition = rotation composition (R2R1 sandwich = composed rotation matrix).
- LEARNING TASK: small rotation-equivariant task - train on rotations near identity, test on large rotations, vs an unstructured MLP baseline; show the equivariance generalization gap (the demo's "why it matters" payoff).
- Rich console output throughout (CLAUDE.md philosophy); runtime < 2 min CPU.

TESTS: add to tests/test_demos.py parametrized module/demo lists; property checks mirrored as assertions in tests/test_mathematical_properties.py (house pattern observed for the other 11).

ACCEPTANCE: `mgr run clifford` clean with all property checks green; `mgr list` shows 12 demos; `mgr info clifford` renders the docstring; tests green.

DEPENDS ON: F1 (design decisions: algebra choice, table construction, normalization).

title: Clifford nanochat attention (production torch implementation)
Hyperbolic-attention framework design doc (framework #13)
description:
WHY: hyperbolic space is the CONTINUOUS hierarchy bias (negative curvature embeds trees with low distortion) where ultrametric is the p-adic DISCRETE one and fractal the self-similar one. Adding it enables the three-way "which hierarchy bias wins" study on hierarchical retrieval (C1 task 3) - the single most interesting comparative question this project can pose, and to our knowledge unrun anywhere.

WHAT: markdown_documentation/hyperbolic_geometry_and_negative_curvature_attention.md in house style:
- MATH: hyperbolic space H^n; model choice analysis - Poincare ball vs Lorentz/hyperboloid. RECOMMEND LORENTZ and justify: distances via Minkowski inner product avoid the Poincare boundary saturation that kills fp32 gradients (cite Nickel & Kiela 2018, "Learning Continuous Hierarchies in the Lorentz Model"); exp/log maps at a basepoint; parallel transport basics.
- MECHANISM DESIGN: q,k mapped to H^n via exp map at the origin; scores = -d_H(q,k)/tau (negative hyperbolic distance); value aggregation via tangent-space approximation of the Frechet mean (log-map values' weights -> weighted average in tangent space -> exp back); curvature c as a LEARNABLE per-head parameter. KEY REDUCTION ANCHOR: as c -> 0 the mechanism must converge to standard (Euclidean) attention - derive the limit; it becomes the F5/F6 acceptance test (the analogue of Clifford's quaternion reduction).
- NUMERICS SECTION (this doc's most important section): fp32 saturation radii; why Lorentz; the constraint manifold <x,x>_L = -1/c maintenance policy (projection after updates); clamping policy with explicit thresholds; bf16 policy (compute Minkowski products in fp32 under autocast).
- POSITIONING: vs existing hyperbolic attention literature (hyperbolic neural networks, HNN++, hyperbolic transformers) - what is different: the certificate-first methodology and the three-way hierarchy-bias comparison.
- PREREGISTERED PREDICTIONS (feeds C3/G1): hyperbolic beats Euclidean standard on hierarchical retrieval at depth >= 4; the three-way ordering question stated as an explicit open hypothesis with adjudication criteria.
- IMPLEMENTATION PLAN sufficient for F5/F6 with zero further design decisions.

ACCEPTANCE: doc complete in house style with the numerics section reviewed extra carefully (this mechanism lives or dies on numerics); predictions registry-ready.

title: Hyperbolic JAX demo with property checks (13th demo)
description:
WHY: house pipeline step 2 for framework #13 - debug the Lorentz-model machinery cheaply, with the property checks that make or break it (numerics) validated interactively before any torch port.

WHAT: new root demo module (file pattern justified as in F2), e.g. hyperbolic_geometry_and_negative_curvature_attention.py, registered as the 13th cli.py DEMOS entry:
- Lorentz-model primitives per F4: Minkowski inner product, d_H, exp/log maps at basepoint, constraint projection.
- PROPERTY CHECKS (rich table, house style):
  1. exp/log inverse round-trip: log(exp(v)) = v within tol across magnitudes (and document where fp32 breaks - that radius goes in the table as a measured value, echoing the F4 numerics section);
  2. Lorentz constraint maintenance: <x,x>_L = -1/c within tol under a short optimization loop;
  3. c -> 0 Euclidean limit: hyperbolic attention output converges to standard attention output as curvature vanishes (sweep c in {1, 0.1, 0.01, 0.001}, show monotone convergence);
  4. tree-embedding sanity (Sarkar-style): embed a random tree, verify distance distortion beats a Euclidean embedding of equal dimension (the classic hyperbolic win, demonstrated concretely);
  5. d_H satisfies the triangle inequality and exceeds Euclidean distance for far-apart points (curvature signature).
- LEARNING TASK: tree reconstruction from learned embeddings vs Euclidean baseline at equal dimension - the canonical demonstration.
- Runtime < 2 min CPU; rich output throughout.

TESTS: tests/test_demos.py registration; property assertions in tests/test_mathematical_properties.py.

ACCEPTANCE: `mgr run hyperbolic` clean, all checks green, 13 demos listed.

DEPENDS ON: F4 (design + numerics policy).
