title: EPIC: Training infrastructure hardening
description:
WHY (background): code investigation found several infrastructure gaps that undermine every experiment built on top:
- nanochat/checkpoint_manager.py is fully implemented (save/load with model_/optim_/meta_ files, per-rank optimizer shards) but ORPHANED - train.py never calls it. Any interrupted run is lost; eval-from-checkpoint and scaling sweeps are impossible.
- No structured per-step metrics stream - only console logs plus an end-of-run summary.json. Dashboards, scaling fits, and automatic verdicts all need step-resolved data.
- hoss_opt_torch.py is a stub compared to the JAX hoss_opt.py (which has symmetric Lanczos curvature via HVP and the analytic OU macro-step with Lyapunov integral). The "3 optimizers" story is currently dishonest in torch.
- scheduler-type only offers none|ordinal: the ordinal scheduler has no modern comparator (cosine/WSD), so its claims are tested against a strawman.
- Zero end-to-end generation/inference CLI - a "production transformer" that has never generated text for a user.
- 29 undocumented env-var feature flags across the JAX demos with no registry or typo protection; no environment diagnostics command for onboarding.

WHAT (scope, see child beads): D1 checkpoint/resume with full state capture; D2 per-step metrics JSONL; D3 HOSS torch parity; D4 optimizer x scheduler bake-off with cosine/WSD baselines; D5 `mgr sample` generation CLI; D6 env-var registry; D7 `mgr doctor`.

STRATEGIC VALUE: converts nanochat from "benchmark script" to "dependable training system". D1 and D2 are hard prerequisites for EP-C (eval-tasks), EP-E (scaling sweeps), and EP-G (verdict engine).
notes: POLISH ADDENDUM: this epic gained an 8th child during the plan-polish pass: rz8.8 (end-to-end pipeline test scripts - full research loop on tiny budgets with rich stage-by-stage logging). It is the program's integration safety net: scenario scripts composing doctor -> gen-tasks -> train (kill + resume) -> certify -> eval-tasks -> sample -> regressions, plus a maintenance contract requiring G2/C4/E1 to append their stages as part of their own acceptance. The child-list sentence in the description above predates it.

title: Checkpoint/resume in train.py (adopt the orphaned checkpoint_manager)
description:
WHY: nanochat/checkpoint_manager.py is fully implemented (save_checkpoint/load_checkpoint, model_{step}.pt / optim_{step}_rank{rank}.pt shards / meta_{step}.json) but train.py NEVER CALLS IT - confirmed by code investigation. Consequences: interrupted runs are lost; C2 eval-tasks has nothing to load; E1 scaling rungs (hours each) are un-runnable; C6 distillation has no teachers. This is the single highest-leverage infrastructure gap in the repo. NOTE: existing bead model_guided_research-1ra (activation checkpointing strategy) is about MEMORY (rematerialization), not persistence - different problem, no overlap.

WHAT: wire checkpoint_manager into train.py IN PLACE:
- Flags: --checkpoint-interval N (steps; 0=off), --checkpoint-dir (default under the run's artifact dir), --checkpoint-keep K (retention), --resume-from {path|latest}.
- FULL state capture: model state_dict; ALL optimizers (AdamW + Muon groups; HOSS state when D3 lands - design the dict layout to accommodate it now); scheduler state (OrdinalScheduler needs state_dict()/load_state_dict() added - A/B/C counters, EMA, best_loss); dataloader state (tokenizing_distributed_data_loader_with_state already returns resume_state_dict with pq_idx/rg_idx - actually plumb it through and restore it); RNG states (torch CPU + CUDA, numpy, python random); step counter; FLOPs-budget accounting so a resumed run honors the ORIGINAL --target-flops (budget consumed so far persisted in meta).
- summary.json records resume lineage: parent run_id chain, resume step.

ACCEPTANCE (the interruption test is the bead):
- Run 100 steps saving at 50; kill; resume -> steps 51-100 loss trajectory matches the uninterrupted run. TARGET: bitwise-identical on CPU fp32 (achievable if RNG + data order restored exactly); if bitwise proves impossible, document precisely why and assert statistical equivalence (same final loss to 1e-4) instead - the WHY must be in the close notes.
- Resume restores: optimizer momenta (verify Muon buffers round-trip), scheduler counters, data position (no repeated/skipped batches - assert via batch-hash logging in the test).
- torch.compile interaction: state_dict key normalization (_orig_mod. prefix) handled both directions; test with --compile on CPU.
- DDP: per-rank optimizer shards save/load correctly (single-rank CI test + code-reviewed rank logic).

CONSUMERS: C2 (eval), C6 (teachers), E1 (rungs), D5 (sampling). Their schemas depend on this layout - document the checkpoint directory contract in checkpoint_manager.py docstring.
notes:
POLISH ADDENDUM (tests & logging):
- SAVE/LOAD LOGGING: every checkpoint save/load logs a rich line: step, path, bytes written/read, duration, components included (model/optim/sched/dataloader/rng) - silent partial saves are the failure mode to design against.
- INTEGRITY OPTION: --checkpoint-verify reloads immediately after save and compares state hashes (model tensors + optimizer tensors), logging the result; cheap corruption tripwire for long runs on flaky storage.
- DIVERGENCE DIAGNOSTICS: the interruption e2e test, on trajectory mismatch, must log the FIRST divergent step with both loss values and the RNG-state comparison at that step - "resume drifted" without a pinpoint is undebuggable.
- RETENTION TEST: --checkpoint-keep K honored (oldest pruned, newest kept, meta files consistent). NOTE: pruning deletes only checkpoint files this feature itself created within the run dir - never user files.
- E2E: this bead's interruption test is promoted to rz8.8 Scenario 2 with randomized kill points (logged seed).

title: Structured per-step metrics stream (JSONL artifact)
description:
WHY: train.py currently emits console logs and ONE end-of-run summary.json. Step-resolved data exists only as ephemeral stdout. Everything analytical downstream - scaling-law fits (E2), verdict adjudication (G2), the planned rich dashboard (existing bead model_guided_research-nyp, which should CONSUME this stream as its data source - coordinate, do not duplicate), regression forensics - needs a durable per-step record.

WHAT: emit one JSONL line per log_interval from train.py into the run's artifact dir alongside summary.json (metrics.jsonl):
{schema_version, step, loss, val_loss (when measured), lr, grad_norm, tokens_per_s, tflops, peak_mem_gb, elapsed_s, and mechanism-specific buffer stats when present: tropical_gamma_min/head_mean, MoM routing entropy + per-member utilization (A3), ordinal scheduler state (rank rho = (A,B,C), restarts), HOSS diagnostics (D3: lanczos eigenvalue extremes)}.
- schema_version field from day one; schema documented in docs/ (extend an existing doc or the artifact conventions doc).
- A small reader utility (function in an existing module, e.g. alongside artifact helpers) with schema validation, used by E2/G2/tests.
- bench-fixed-flops subprocess runs inherit it automatically (it is just train.py).

PERFORMANCE: buffered writes, flush at val intervals and exit (no per-line fsync); rank-0 only under DDP; measured overhead < 1% step time on CPU smoke config (record measurement in close notes).

ACCEPTANCE:
- metrics.jsonl present for all training paths incl. subprocess benchmarks; final line flushed on clean exit AND on KeyboardInterrupt (try/finally).
- Reader round-trip test on a real tiny run; malformed-line tolerance (skip with warning, never crash an analysis over one bad line).
- Buffer-stat passthrough tested for tropical (gamma stats present when --tropical-record-margins).

title: HOSS PyTorch parity with the JAX implementation
description:
WHY: the README sells three optimizers, but hoss_opt_torch.py is a minimal stub while the JAX hoss_opt.py implements the real algorithm: symmetric Lanczos curvature estimation via Hessian-vector products, then an analytic Ornstein-Uhlenbeck macro-step over delta with the Lyapunov integral computed by eigendecomposition. Framework #11 (nonstandard analysis / hyperreal training) therefore has NO production-grade torch realization, and every torch optimizer comparison that includes HOSS is currently misleading. D4's bake-off is blocked on this being honest.

WHAT: port the full algorithm to hoss_opt_torch.py IN PLACE:
- HVP via double-backprop (torch.func or autograd.grad twice) - requires a LOSS CLOSURE, so HOSS cannot be a pure .step() optimizer. Design the closure interface deliberately (LBFGS-style closure convention), plumb it through train.py's step path, and DOCUMENT why (this is an API decision others will read).
- k-step symmetric Lanczos (k ~ 10, configurable) building the tridiagonal T; eigendecompose; compute phi_delta and the Lyapunov integral EXACTLY as the JAX version does - mirror the math comments line-for-line so the two implementations stay reviewable side-by-side.
- Shaped noise injection per the SDE solution; all states stored per-param-group for D1 checkpointing (coordinate the state dict layout with D1).
- Keep the existing guard: incompatible with gpt_synaptic (no closure available there).

VALIDATION (the bead's core):
1. Reproduce the stiff-quadratic demo from nonstandard_analysis_and_hyperreal_training.py (run_stiff_quadratic_demo) in torch: matching convergence behavior across a 100x learning-rate sweep - the LR-robustness claim is THE claim; this becomes a B1-style certificate.
2. JAX<->torch numerical cross-check: identical synthetic problem, seed-controlled identical Lanczos starting vector -> extreme eigenvalue estimates agree within 1e-4.
3. HVP correctness vs explicit Hessian on a tiny MLP (fp64).

ACCEPTANCE:
- nanochat trains end-to-end with --optimizer-type hoss on CPU smoke config, no nonfinite grads (--check-numerics clean).
- Honest overhead documented: wall-clock factor vs AdamW at equal steps AND equal wall-clock comparison guidance for D4.
- Unit tests for Lanczos (known-spectrum matrix), Lyapunov integral (closed-form 1D case), and closure plumbing.
notes:
POLISH ADDENDUM (tests & logging):
- DIAGNOSTICS INTO METRICS STREAM: emit per-log_interval HOSS diagnostics into the D2 metrics.jsonl: Lanczos extreme eigenvalue estimates (lambda_min/lambda_max), chosen step scaling, injected noise scale - D2's schema already reserves these fields; a test asserts presence and finiteness during a smoke run.
- STEP LOGGING: optional debug flag logging per-step Lanczos convergence (iterations, residual) - curvature estimation failures must be visible, not silent.
- CROSS-IMPL TEST LOGGING: the JAX<->torch cross-check test logs both eigenvalue sequences side-by-side on failure (not just the max-diff scalar) so numerical divergence is localizable.
- CLOSURE-API TESTS: closure called the expected number of times per step; helpful TypeError when train.py passes no closure with --optimizer-type hoss; gpt_synaptic guard still raises with a clear message.

title: Optimizer x scheduler bake-off with modern baselines (cosine/WSD)
description:
WHY: --scheduler-type currently offers none|ordinal. Beating "none" is a strawman: the ordinal scheduler's transfinite restart story (omega^2*A + omega*B + C) means nothing until it faces cosine and WSD (warmup-stable-decay), the actual modern defaults. Likewise the optimizer story (adamw/muon/hoss) has never been compared under one controlled protocol in this repo.

WHAT:
1. Add cosine and WSD schedulers to train.py IN PLACE (--scheduler-type none|ordinal|cosine|wsd; trivial math, golden-curve unit tests: LR value at known step fractions).
2. Fixed-FLOPs bake-off: {adamw, muon, hoss} x {none, cosine, wsd, ordinal} x >=3 seeds, on standard attention plus two exotic mechanisms (tropical, ultrametric) to detect optimizer-mechanism interactions (plausible: max-plus subgradients may want different momentum handling).
3. ORDINAL'S REAL TEST: the regime-shift stream (C1 task 7): measure steps-to-recover pre-shift loss after each boundary. Restarts are the ordinal scheduler's selling point; stationary FineWeb cannot show it. Report restart counts and post-restart recovery curves from the D2 metrics stream (ordinal state is logged there).
4. LR FAIRNESS: optimizers need different LR scales - run a coarse 3-point LR sweep per (optimizer, scheduler) cell and report the best, rather than sharing one LR; document the protocol in the report (this is the most common bake-off methodology error; do not commit it).

OUTPUT: artifacts per conventions; statistical comparison via the existing Mann-Whitney machinery; explicit verdict lines: "ordinal {beats|matches|loses to} cosine on stationary data" and "... under regime shift"; verdicts feed the G1 registry (the ordinal claims are registry entries via C3).

RELATED: existing bead model_guided_research-2bx (config templates for fair comparisons) - reuse if landed; existing model_guided_research-u8l (HP search plan) is broader - this bead is the focused scheduler/optimizer question.

DEPENDS ON: D2 (metrics stream for recovery curves), D3 (honest HOSS). C1 task 7 used when available - if C1 has not landed, build the regime-shift stream as a minimal inline generator and note the duplication for C1 to absorb (do not block).

ACCEPTANCE: report committed; scheduler unit tests green; every cell's best-LR protocol recorded in summary.json.

title: mgr sample: end-to-end generation CLI
Centralize demo env-var/feature-flag registry with validation
description:
WHY: code investigation counted 29 distinct environment variables steering the JAX demos (GAUGE_* x9, TROP_* x8, REV_* x6, ULTRA_* x5, BRAID_CROSSING_LAW) with no central registry, no validation, and no discoverability. A typo (GAUGE_STRUCTRED=1) is silently ignored - the worst failure mode for research code because the experiment silently runs the WRONG configuration and the result gets believed.

WHAT:
1. Declarative registry in config.py IN PLACE (AGENTS.md: no new file): a table of {name, demo, type (bool/int/float/enum), default, description} covering all 29 flags.
2. Helper get_flag(name) used by the demos - the call-site migration is many simple mechanical changes: per AGENTS.md, parallelize across subagents for the simple swaps; NO regex scripting.
3. `mgr flags [demo]` CLI command: rich table of flags, types, defaults, current values, descriptions (discoverability).
4. Validation at demo start: warn loudly (rich yellow panel) on any set env var that prefix-matches a known demo family (GAUGE_/TROP_/REV_/ULTRA_/BRAID_) but is not in the registry - catches typos; also validate value types (non-integer in an int flag = hard error with the expected format).

BEHAVIOR GUARANTEE: zero behavior change for correctly-spelled flags - this is pure relocation + validation. Defaults must be byte-identical to current behavior (capture current defaults in a test BEFORE migrating).

ACCEPTANCE:
- All 29 flags in the registry with descriptions; `mgr flags` renders the table;
- Typo test: set GAUGE_STRUCTRED=1, run gauge demo, assert warning emitted;
- Type test: ULTRA_PACKED=banana -> hard error with helpful message;
- Demo equivalence: one demo run per family before/after migration with flags set, identical outputs (seeded).

title: mgr doctor: environment diagnostics command
End-to-end pipeline test scripts: full research loop on tiny budgets, with rich stage-by-stage logging
description:
WHY: every bead in this program carries its own unit/parity/golden tests, but nothing exercises the FULL research loop as a user would run it. Integration failures (artifact schema drift between commands, checkpoint format mismatches, CLI flag renames, tokenizer/dataloader incompatibilities) only surface where commands COMPOSE - and right now nothing composes them automatically. This bead is the program's integration safety net and the single highest-value test asset for catching cross-bead regressions during the long implementation campaign ahead.

WHAT: e2e scenario scripts under scripts/ (precedent: scripts/verify_flex_correctness.py), runnable locally and as an optional CI job (manual/nightly trigger - too slow for per-PR), each completing on CPU in bounded time:

SCENARIO 1 - FULL LOOP (<15 min CPU): mgr doctor (optional stage - skip with logged warning if not yet implemented) -> mgr gen-tasks (one small diagnostic task) -> train standard + tropical at tiny --target-flops with --checkpoint-interval -> SIGKILL one run mid-training -> resume it and verify completion -> mgr certify (fast subset) -> mgr eval-tasks on both checkpoints -> mgr sample (greedy, fixed seed) -> mgr regressions comparing the two variants' summaries. Every artifact consumed by a later stage was produced by an earlier one - that chain IS the test.
SCENARIO 2 - RESUME ROBUSTNESS: SIGKILL at a randomized step (logged seed), resume, assert trajectory continuity per the D1 acceptance criteria (this is D1's interruption test promoted to a repeatable script with randomized kill points).
SCENARIO 3 - GENERATION DETERMINISM: greedy same-seed decode across every KV-cache-supporting mechanism; byte-identical outputs across two invocations; tokens/s recorded per mechanism.
SCENARIO 4 - BENCHMARK + REGRESSION GATE: bench-fixed-flops with two variants at tiny budget -> mgr regressions --fail-on-regression against a committed golden baseline snapshot; proves the gate trips on a deliberately degraded fixture.

LOGGING REQUIREMENTS (the user-facing core of this bead - logs must make any failure diagnosable WITHOUT rerunning):
- every stage wrapped in rich BEGIN/END panels: stage name, key params, elapsed time, artifact paths produced;
- on stage failure: dump the tail (last ~50 lines) of the failing subprocess's output, the env snapshot (mgr doctor --json if available, else a minimal inline probe), and the exact reproduction command - all into the report;
- consolidated e2e_report.md + e2e_report.json artifact per run (stage matrix: pass/fail/skipped, durations, artifact inventory with sizes);
- nonzero exit on any required-stage failure; skipped optional stages reported as SKIPPED, never silently absent.

MAINTENANCE CONTRACT (binding on later beads, noted on each): G2 (adjudicate stage), C4 (scorecard smoke cell), and E1 (smoke ladder) must extend Scenario 1 with their stage as part of their OWN acceptance criteria - this script is append-only infrastructure, never allowed to go stale.

CI INTEGRATION: workflow_dispatch + nightly schedule job in ci.yml (separate from the fast PR jobs); uploads e2e_report artifacts on failure. RELATED: existing bead model_guided_research-c6v (CI-lite uv workflow) - the local invocation should be the same entrypoint c6v wraps.

ACCEPTANCE: all four scenarios green on CPU from a fresh clone (after uv sync + data fixture); deliberate-breakage check during development for each scenario (e.g. corrupt a checkpoint file, change an artifact field name) confirms the failure is caught AND the log/report pinpoints the failing stage and shows the repro command; total runtime documented per scenario.

DEPENDS ON: B1/5ki.1 (certify), C2/vdc.2 (eval-tasks; transitively C1 tasks + D1 checkpoints), D2/rz8.2 (metrics assertions), D5/rz8.5 (sample). mgr doctor (rz8.7) deliberately NOT a dependency - used opportunistically.

title: Shared config parity tests (bio_inspired ↔ MGR)
description:
- Define a small battery of parity tests/configs runnable in both repos (or replicated here) to compare baseline/Flex/feature toggles under matched hyperparameters.
- Capture command lines, seeds, and expected metrics to detect drift over time.
- Store configs/commands in docs/config_parity_suite.md; align with FLOPs harness and baseline tasks.
acceptance_criteria: Config parity suite defined with ≥3 matched runs (baseline, Flex, one math feature) including commands, seeds, expected metric bands; runnable here (and/or mirrored instructions for bio_inspired); documented in docs/config_parity_suite.md.

title: Investigate torch.compile failure for synaptic FlexAttention KV-cache paths
description: On torch 2.9.1+cu128, torch.compile of synaptic FlexAttention fails in KV-cache paths (verify_flex_correctness --model synaptic --compile), with Inductor/SymInt errors (e.g., AttributeError: 'int' object has no attribute 'is_Add' while lowering torch.sym_max) and earlier FlexibleLayout indexing failures. Investigate minimal repro and implement a workaround or document a compatibility matrix.
acceptance_criteria: Repro captured (exact torch/cuda/flags) and either: (a) synaptic FlexAttention compiles for full_forward + KV-cache paths, or (b) clearly documented as unsupported with version constraints + skip logic in scripts/tests.
close_reason: Fix: use torch.compile(dynamic=False) for synaptic FlexAttention checks to avoid Inductor SymInt lowering failure in KV-cache paths; verified verify_flex_correctness --model synaptic --compile now passes.

title: EPIC: Reach & versatility - standalone API and documentation site
description:
WHY (background): the work only compounds if others can USE it. Two adoption funnels are currently closed:
1. The attention mechanisms cannot be cleanly imported into someone else's PyTorch model - they are coupled to GPTConfig and live behind training-oriented modules. "pip install, three lines, tropical attention in YOUR transformer" is the cheapest possible adoption funnel and turns the repo from "a benchmark you read about" into "a library you try". (Depends on EP-A's A1: the refactored scaffolding defines the stable public surface worth documenting.)
2. The 338KB of graduate-level mathematics in markdown_documentation/ (24-45KB per framework, with derivations and proofs) is the project's most undervalued asset - and it is effectively unreadable as raw GitHub markdown blobs (no LaTeX rendering, no navigation). A static docs site with per-framework pages linking theory -> JAX demo -> torch implementation -> certificates -> scorecard verdicts makes the full pipeline visible per framework; that page structure IS the project pitch.

WHAT (scope, see child beads): H1 standalone public API + wheel-install CI test; H2 static docs site (MkDocs Material or equivalent, decided in-bead) with math rendering and GitHub Pages deploy.

RELATED EXISTING BEADS: model_guided_research-9f1 (pedagogical walkthrough mode) is the interactive in-terminal pedagogy angle; H2 is the web angle - coordinate content structure, do not duplicate. The release.yml PyPI workflow already exists; H1 adds the proof that the published wheel actually works standalone.

title: Standalone public API for attention mechanisms (+ wheel-install CI proof)
description:
WHY: "pip install and drop TropicalAttention into YOUR model" is the cheapest adoption funnel this project can build, and adoption is what converts the work from a private benchmark into a cited library. Today the mechanisms are entangled with GPTConfig and training-module imports; the release.yml workflow publishes a wheel nobody has verified works standalone.

WHAT:
1. PUBLIC SURFACE: define __all__ exports in nanochat/__init__.py for every mechanism class; ensure mechanism modules import cleanly in a torch-only environment (no JAX leakage through transitive imports - audit and fix import graphs IN PLACE).
2. CONSTRUCTION ERGONOMICS: a from_dims(n_embd, n_head, **mechanism_kwargs) classmethod on the A1 scaffold (so external users never touch GPTConfig), with per-mechanism kwargs documented in docstrings. The A1 protocol should already minimize GPTConfig coupling - this bead finishes the job. Do NOT fork modules to decouple (AGENTS.md); fix imports in place.
3. CONTRACT DOCS: input/output shapes, mask semantics, KV-cache optionality, dtype expectations - documented in the README new section "Using the mechanisms in your own model" with a runnable example (tropical attention inside a vanilla PyTorch transformer block, ~20 lines).
4. SMOKE TESTS: build each mechanism standalone via from_dims without GPTConfig; forward + backward on random input; run in CI.
5. WHEEL PROOF: CI job (extend ci.yml): uv build -> install the wheel into a FRESH venv -> python -c import + tiny forward for 3 representative mechanisms. This catches packaging rot (missing modules, data files, version pins) that repo-checkout testing never sees.

ACCEPTANCE: README section live; standalone smoke tests green; wheel-install CI job green on main; zero new code files (docs + tests + in-place edits only).

DEPENDS ON: A1 (the refactored scaffold IS the stable public surface; documenting the pre-refactor one would be wasted work).

title: Static documentation site (theory docs + pipeline pages + artifact showcases)
description:
WHY: markdown_documentation/ holds 338KB of graduate-level mathematics (11 docs, 24-45KB each, with derivations and proofs) - the project's most undervalued asset, currently unreadable in practice (raw GitHub markdown blobs: no LaTeX rendering, no navigation, no cross-linking). A docs site also gives the scorecard/scaling results a public home.

WHAT:
1. TOOLING DECISION (make in-implementation, document the rationale in the site config): MkDocs Material is the default recommendation (lowest friction for math-heavy markdown, KaTeX/MathJax plugin, search built in); alternatives considered: Sphinx (heavier), custom Nextra (overkill without a webapp need).
2. CONTENT - render, do not rewrite:
   - the 11 (13 after EP-F) theory docs AS-IS, fixing only render-breaking syntax (math delimiters, etc.);
   - a quickstart distilled from the README;
   - PER-FRAMEWORK PIPELINE PAGES - the site's centerpiece and the project's pitch made visible: for each framework one page linking theory doc -> JAX demo (how to run, what the property checks verify) -> torch implementation (file, flags, constraints) -> certificates (B1 results when available) -> scorecard verdicts (C4/G2 ledger rows when available). Pages degrade gracefully when later-stage artifacts do not exist yet ("not yet adjudicated" rather than broken links).
   - artifact showcases: the scaling overlay plot (E2), the scorecard (C4), the hypothesis ledger (G2) - embedded/linked as they materialize.
3. DEPLOY: GitHub Pages via a new workflow file (.github/workflows/ - infrastructure, not code proliferation); build on push to main; site URL added to README and the repo's About field.
4. MATH RENDERING ACCEPTANCE: spot-check the heaviest pages - the BCH formula sections in matrix_exponential_gauge_learning.md and the p-adic notation in ultrametric_worlds - render correctly.

RELATED: existing bead model_guided_research-9f1 (pedagogical walkthrough mode) is the in-terminal pedagogy angle - share the per-framework content structure (theory/demo/production/verdict) so the two stay consistent; do not duplicate prose.

ACCEPTANCE: site builds in CI and deploys; math renders on the spot-checked pages; per-framework pipeline pages live for all current frameworks; README links the site.

NO HARD DEPENDENCIES (renders whatever exists; richer as other epics land).

title: HP search plan (CMA-ES / grid)
description:
- Draft a lightweight plan for hyperparameter sweeps under fixed FLOPs using CMA-ES or grid (see bio_inspired_nanochat plan doc). Identify search spaces for Flex vs standard, math features.
- Specify budget, metrics, and how to capture results to artifacts.
- No code yet; document in docs/hparam_search_plan.md.

title: Unified artifacts logging layout
description:
- Define a consistent artifacts directory structure (benchmarks, correctness, profiles, certs) and ensure CLI/scripts write JSON+markdown summaries accordingly.
- Update existing scripts to honor the layout without breaking current paths; add README describing conventions.
- Include pointer to FLOPs-budget harness outputs and flex/standard comparisons.
acceptance_criteria: Artifacts directory layout defined and documented; existing/pending scripts updated to target it or migration plan written; includes subdirs for baseline, bench, perf, profiles, certs; naming conventions clarified.
close_reason: Unified artifacts layout documented in artifacts/README.md; scripts/CLI now emit JSON+MD into artifacts/{baseline,bench,perf,certs} and nanochat baseline writes under artifacts/baseline/nanochat/

title: Per-attention-type hyperparameter sensitivity analysis
description: Each exotic attention type may need different hyperparameters: (1) Test LR sensitivity per attention type, (2) Check batch size effects, (3) Verify gradient clipping needs, (4) Document recommended HP ranges per type, (5) Create config templates. Unfair comparison if vanilla uses optimal HPs but exotic uses defaults.
close_reason: Redundant with existing 2bx (Nanochat config templates for fair comparisons). HP sensitivity should be documented in config templates.

title: EPIC: Theory-aligned task battery & falsifiable scorecards
description:
WHY (background): The README's "Expected Performance Characteristics" table (tropical->robustness, ultrametric->long sequences/hierarchy, simplicial->multi-entity reasoning, quaternion->3D/spatial, braid->permutation/composition, fractal->recursion, surreal->wide dynamic range, reversible->memory, gauge->stability) is currently pure conjecture: nanochat only measures LM loss and throughput on FineWeb-Edu, which is the one regime where NONE of the exotic biases should obviously matter. tests/test_practical_utility.py covers demo-scale toy versions only.

WHAT (scope, see child beads):
1. A synthetic diagnostic task suite generator - tasks designed so each mechanism's theoretical advantage should manifest as a measurable gap (Dyck-k, hierarchical retrieval, multi-entity relations, rotation composition, wide-dynamic-range arithmetic, regime shifts, needle-in-haystack), emitted as parquet compatible with the existing dataloader.
2. A trained-checkpoint eval harness (`mgr eval-tasks`) with exact-match metrics and accuracy-vs-length/depth extrapolation curves.
3. A preregistered mechanism<->task prediction matrix (encoded in the EP-G hypothesis registry BEFORE running - git history is the timestamp).
4. The scorecard run: all mechanisms x relevant tasks at fixed FLOPs, multi-seed, producing a single predicted-vs-observed verdict report (SUPPORTED / REFUTED / INCONCLUSIVE).
5. Length/depth extrapolation study and (later) distillation/transplant controls separating representational from optimization effects.

STRATEGIC VALUE: this is the project's scientific heart - it converts "exotic math is pretty" into "exotic math measurably wins/loses HERE, at this scale, with CIs". A scorecard with honest REFUTED rows is more credible (and more publishable) than a wall of wins.

RELATED EXISTING BEADS: model_guided_research-qq7 (math-feature ablations) is within-mechanism knob ablation; this epic is cross-mechanism task evaluation - share harness patterns, do not duplicate.

title: Synthetic diagnostic task suite generator (theory-aligned datasets)
description:
WHY: to falsify the README's mechanism-advantage claims we need tasks DESIGNED so each mechanism's theoretical edge should manifest as a measurable gap. FineWeb-Edu LM loss cannot do this. The JAX demos contain toy versions (Dyck in the braid demo, retrieval in ultrametric); this bead builds the nanochat-scale data side.

WHAT: a generator emitting tokenized parquet datasets compatible with nanochat/dataloader.py UNCHANGED (same schema FineWeb parquet uses: a text column consumed by document_batches). One new module is justified (genuinely new functionality): keep it to ONE file, e.g. nanochat/diagnostics_data.py. CLI: `mgr gen-tasks --task X --out DIR --size N --seed S [--difficulty ...]`.

THE TASK LIST (the spec - each entry names its target mechanism(s) and hypothesis):
1. Dyck-k balanced brackets - braid/topology: validity classification + completion; length-generalization on nesting depth (the braid JAX demo's Dyck experiments are the precedent).
2. copy / reverse / rotate sequences - braid (permutation handling), reversible (information preservation).
3. hierarchical retrieval - ultrametric/fractal/[later hyperbolic]: nested key-value trees serialized to text; query values at depth d; held-out DEPTHS, not just held-out samples. LCP-style routing should shine here if it works at all.
4. multi-entity relation queries - simplicial: tuples (A rel B) in context, query 2-hop compositions (who is connected to X via Y?); triangle aggregation is the predicted advantage.
5. 3D rotation composition - quaternion/octonion/[later clifford]: serialized rotation sequences (axis-angle tokens), predict the composed orientation bucket.
6. wide-dynamic-range arithmetic - surreal: compare/order numbers spanning 1e-6..1e6 in scientific notation; magnitude-direction separation is the predicted advantage.
7. regime-shift streams - ordinal scheduler + HOSS: corpus whose distribution flips at known boundaries; tests restart/recovery behavior (consumed by D4 too).
8. needle-in-haystack at lengths 256..4096 - ultrametric (sub-quadratic + hierarchy) and long-context behavior generally.

REQUIREMENTS:
- Deterministic from seed: same seed -> byte-identical parquet (unit-tested).
- Train/val/test splits with HELD-OUT LENGTHS AND DEPTHS (extrapolation is the point; in-distribution accuracy alone is uninformative).
- Brute-force label checkers per task (used by tests AND by C2 metrics as ground truth).
- Per-task docstring: which mechanism(s), which hypothesis, why this design elicits it - this text seeds the C3 preregistered predictions.

PITFALLS: BPE tokenization can destroy structure (merging across brackets/delimiters). Verify token boundaries respect task delimiters; if not, use explicit delimiter tokens known to the tokenizer. Test this explicitly per task.

ACCEPTANCE: generated parquet loads through the existing dataloader untouched; determinism, split-disjointness, and label-correctness tests pass; `mgr gen-tasks --task all` builds the full battery in < 10 min CPU.
notes:
POLISH ADDENDUM (tests & logging):
- PER-DATASET MANIFEST: write manifest.json next to each generated dataset: {task, seed, generator params, split sizes, sha256 of each parquet file, generator code version}. The checksums make the determinism test trivial (same seed -> same hashes) and detect silent drift when generator code changes (version bump required to change hashes - enforce in test).
- GENERATION LOGGING: rich per-task summary table on generation: example count, token-length stats (min/median/max), label/answer distribution, split sizes, held-out length/depth ranges - reviewers must be able to sanity-check a dataset from the log without opening parquet.
- TOKENIZER-SAFETY TEST is per-task and explicit: assert delimiter tokens survive round-trip encode/decode unmerged for EVERY task, not just spot-checked ones.
- E2E: gen-tasks is stage 2 of the rz8.8 pipeline script.

title: Trained-checkpoint eval harness (mgr eval-tasks)
description:
WHY: nanochat has no evaluation beyond LM loss. The diagnostic battery (C1) needs task metrics computed on TRAINED models, which needs checkpoint loading (D1).

WHAT: `mgr eval-tasks --checkpoint DIR --task X [--all-tasks] [--device] [--seeds ...]`:
- loads a nanochat checkpoint via D1's manager,
- runs task-specific metrics: exact-match accuracy (retrieval, arithmetic, rotation buckets), Dyck validity rate, per-position accuracy curves, and the headline EXTRAPOLATION CURVES: accuracy-vs-length and accuracy-vs-depth (in-range vs held-out ranges marked),
- per-task perplexity as a secondary metric,
- greedy AND sampled decoding via the existing generate() (gpt.py); answers parsed by the C1 brute-force checkers (decode -> strip -> canonicalize before compare; tokenizer quirks must not fail correct answers - test this),
- multi-seed aggregation with mean +/- CI,
- writes summary.json + run.md per the established artifact conventions (cli.py layout).

SCHEMA: the JSON output schema is a CONTRACT - documented, versioned (schema_version field), consumed by C4 scorecards and the G2 verdict engine. Changing it later requires a version bump and migration note.

MECHANISM COVERAGE: must work for all mechanisms with KV-cache decode, including trie-mode ultrametric (CPU decode path) and simplicial (y1 cache). Gauge is excluded with a clear error until A5 lands - do not fake it.

ACCEPTANCE:
- End-to-end on a tiny CPU-trained checkpoint in < 5 min (CI-lite friendly).
- Golden-fixture test: a committed tiny checkpoint fixture under tests/ with KNOWN scores; eval-tasks must reproduce them exactly (catches metric drift).
- Curves rendered (matplotlib, already a dep) and embedded/linked in run.md.

DEPENDS ON: C1 (tasks + checkers), D1 (checkpoint loading).
notes:
POLISH ADDENDUM (tests & logging):
- VERBOSE FAILURE LOGGING: --verbose logs per-example failures (prompt, expected, got, decoded token ids) capped at N per task (default ~10) - eval debugging must not require a separate instrumentation pass.
- RUN HEADER: log checkpoint metadata pulled from D1 meta (mechanism, n_params, training budget, parent run lineage) so every eval artifact is self-identifying.
- METRIC UNIT TESTS: each task metric tested against hand-computed values on tiny fixtures (including parser canonicalization edge cases: leading whitespace, trailing EOS, tokenizer merge quirks).
- E2E: eval-tasks is a stage of the rz8.8 pipeline script; the golden-fixture checkpoint with KNOWN scores is the drift tripwire - any metric change must update the fixture deliberately with a justification note.

title: Preregistered mechanism-task prediction matrix
description:
WHY: post-hoc storytelling is the credibility killer of ML benchmarking. Predictions must be committed BEFORE the scorecard runs - git history is the timestamp. This bead encodes the README "Expected Performance Characteristics" claims as machine-readable preregistered predictions in the EP-G registry.

WHAT: for each claim, a registry entry (G1 schema): {mechanism, task (C1 id), metric (exact path into the C2 JSON schema), comparator/direction, minimum effect size, baseline = standard attention at equal FLOPs, scale caveats, seeds required}. Write ALL of them (~15+); examples that MUST be included:
- ultrametric beats standard on hierarchical-retrieval exact-match at depth>=4 by >=5 points at equal FLOPs.
- tropical degrades at most half as much as standard under eps-bounded input perturbation (robustness probe defined alongside).
- braid generalizes Dyck-k to 2x training length with a smaller accuracy drop than standard (delta-of-deltas).
- quaternion beats standard on rotation-composition by >=3 points at equal FLOPs.
- fractal beats standard on hierarchical retrieval at held-out depths (extrapolation, not in-range).
- simplicial beats standard on 2-hop relation queries by >=3 points.
- surreal beats standard on wide-dynamic-range comparison accuracy.
- reversible matches standard within 2% final loss while peak training memory <= 60%.
- ordinal scheduler re-attains pre-shift loss >=30% faster than cosine after a regime shift (uses C1 task 7; coordinates with D4).
- ultrametric long-context: needle-in-haystack at T=4096 with measured wall-clock advantage (coordinates with E3).

FAIRNESS REVIEW (part of this bead): each prediction reviewed for equal-FLOPs comparability, seed count >=3, and a metric the C2 schema actually exposes. A prediction nobody can adjudicate is a bug.

ACCEPTANCE: entries validate against the G1 schema (mgr hypotheses validate); each names its exact metric path; committed and pushed BEFORE any C4 scorecard run (enforce by ordering: C4 depends on this bead).

DEPENDS ON: C1 (task ids/definitions), G1 (registry schema + CLI).

title: Scorecard run: full battery x all mechanisms, predicted-vs-observed verdicts
description:
WHY: the headline deliverable of the entire benchmarking effort - one artifact a researcher can read in five minutes that says which mathematical structures earned their keep, on which tasks, at what scale, with what uncertainty.

WHAT: orchestrated run via a new `mgr scorecard --budget <flops> [--mechanisms ...] [--tasks ...] [--seeds N]` command:
1. For each mechanism (11 + standard baseline; plus the best A4 hybrid when available): train at fixed FLOPs on each task relevant to it per the C3 matrix (small budgets, e.g. 2e9 CPU smoke to 2e10+ GPU; configurable).
2. Run C2 eval-tasks on every resulting checkpoint, >=3 seeds.
3. Generate THE SCORECARD: markdown + JSON (+ HTML reusing the regressions HTML/CSS machinery in cli.py) with one row per preregistered prediction: SUPPORTED / REFUTED / INCONCLUSIVE (CI overlaps threshold) / BLOCKED (qualifying runs impossible - e.g. gauge pre-A5, octonion pre-A6 at large T; mark blocked rather than silently dropping rows).
Verdict assignment itself is delegated to the G2 engine when available; until then compute inline with the same statistical policy (CI-aware) and emit registry-compatible JSON so G2 can re-adjudicate identically later.

STATISTICS: multi-seed mean +/- CI; effect sizes; Mann-Whitney U reused from cli.py per-head-metrics machinery. Both analytic FLOPs and measured wall-clock reported per cell (relates existing bead model_guided_research-bks).

RELATED: existing bead model_guided_research-qq7 (math-feature ablations) is within-mechanism; share harness plumbing. Existing bead model_guided_research-2bx (config templates for fair comparisons) - use its templates if landed.

ACCEPTANCE:
- Complete scorecard artifact for >=8 mechanisms committed under artifacts/ and linked from README.
- Reproducible from one documented command; total runtime documented.
- At least one REFUTED or INCONCLUSIVE row honestly reported (if everything comes back SUPPORTED, treat that as a methodology red flag and re-review the fairness of budgets/seeds before publishing).

DEPENDS ON: C2 (eval harness), C3 (preregistered predictions locked first).
notes:
POLISH ADDENDUM (feature + tests & logging):
- ORCHESTRATION RESUME (functional requirement, not just logging): the scorecard run maintains a manifest.json over its (mechanism x task x seed) cell matrix with per-cell status (pending/running/done/failed) updated atomically (tmp+rename); interrupting and rerunning `mgr scorecard` skips completed cells. Same pattern as E1's sweep manifest - a multi-hour orchestration without resume is operationally unusable.
- LIVE PROGRESS: rich live table over the cell matrix (cell, status, elapsed, loss-so-far); per-cell subprocess logs persisted under the run dir and referenced from the report on failure.
- TESTS: manifest resume unit test (kill after k cells, rerun, assert exactly the remaining cells execute); deliberate-cell-failure test (one cell fails -> scorecard completes other cells, marks the cell FAILED in the report, exit code reflects partial failure).
- MAINTENANCE CONTRACT: add a scorecard smoke cell (1 mechanism x 1 task x tiny budget) to the rz8.8 e2e pipeline script as part of this bead's acceptance.

title: Length & depth extrapolation study (train short, eval long)
description:
WHY: the sharpest falsifiable versions of the structural claims are about EXTRAPOLATION: ultrametric tree addressing, fractal self-similar routing, and braid topological invariants all predict better out-of-training-range generalization than standard attention's learned positional patterns. RoPE-based standard attention is a strong, honest baseline (RoPE extrapolates somewhat) - beating a strawman is worthless.

WHAT: train each mechanism at T=256 on C1 tasks (1) Dyck-k, (3) hierarchical retrieval, (8) needle-in-haystack; evaluate at T in {256, 512, 1024, 2048, 4096} and retrieval depth in {2..8}; produce per-mechanism degradation curves (accuracy vs length/depth, training range marked) and include them in the C4 scorecard.

TECHNICAL REQUIREMENTS:
- sequence_len is baked into GPTConfig and the RoPE cos/sin cache - eval-time context extension must be EXPLICIT: recompute/extend the rotary cache for the eval length; document the policy (no interpolation tricks in v1 - plain extension; note NTK/PI scaling as future work).
- Any mechanism with learned absolute structure that cannot honestly run longer than trained must be FLAGGED in the report, not silently truncated.
- Ultrametric trie decode at long T is CPU-only - measure but annotate device.
- Memory: T=4096 eval on the larger configs may need batch-1 eval paths; handle gracefully.

ACCEPTANCE:
- Curves artifact (matplotlib) + JSON per the C2 schema extension; a written conclusion paragraph per mechanism in the report (supported/refuted/inconclusive on its extrapolation prediction);
- Verdicts flow into the G1 registry via the C3 entries that reference held-out lengths/depths.

DEPENDS ON: C2 (eval harness; C1 arrives transitively).

title: Representation-vs-optimization controls: distillation & weight transplant
description:
WHY: when a mechanism wins on the scorecard, the immediate skeptical question is: better REPRESENTATION, or just better-conditioned OPTIMIZATION on this budget? Two classic controls disambiguate, and almost nobody runs them - doing so is a credibility differentiator.

WHAT:
(a) DISTILLATION CONTROL: add a KD option to train.py IN PLACE: --distill-from <checkpoint> --distill-temp <T> --distill-alpha <a> (KL(student||teacher-softened) blended with LM loss). Train exotic-student from standard-teacher and standard-student from the same teacher under identical budgets. If exotic-student > standard-student under identical distillation signal, the advantage is representational (the optimization path was equalized by the teacher).
(b) TRANSPLANT CONTROL: `mgr transplant --src CKPT --dst-config ... --map embeddings,mlp[,attn-where-shapes-match]`: initialize an exotic model with weights transplanted from a trained standard model (the A1 shared scaffolding makes embeddings/MLP/lm_head transplant well-defined; attention projections transplant where shapes coincide, else re-init). Then short fine-tune. If the exotic advantage persists from transplanted init, it is not an artifact of the exotic-specific early-training trajectory.

SCOPE CONTROL: run on the 2-3 TOP mechanisms from the C4 scorecard only (this is deliberately p3 - it has no value until C4 produces winners; the train.py KD plumbing can be built earlier if convenient).

ACCEPTANCE:
- KD path unit-tested (loss decreases on smoke config; gradients flow; teacher in eval mode, no teacher grads).
- Transplant utility tested (key mapping correct, shape mismatches handled with explicit report of what transplanted vs re-initialized).
- Short report: 2x2 cells (mechanism x {scratch, distilled}) + transplant trajectories, with an explicit representational-vs-optimization conclusion per mechanism, fed into the G1 registry as evidence.

DEPENDS ON: D1 (checkpoints for teachers/transplant sources). Soft-depends on C4 results for mechanism selection (do not hard-block: harness can be built and smoke-tested before C4 finishes).

title: EPIC: Scaling-law study across mathematical frameworks
description:
WHY (background): README Open Question #1 asks "Do advantages persist at GPT-3/4 scale?" - unanswerable directly on this project's compute, but scaling LAWS are the honest proxy: fit loss-vs-compute curves per mechanism over an affordable model-size ladder and compare exponents and intercepts. A mechanism with a better scaling exponent at small scale is evidence its advantage GROWS with scale; a flat or worse exponent is an equally publishable negative result. Nobody has published scaling-law comparisons for tropical/ultrametric/quaternion/braid/fractal attention - this is genuinely open territory and the result most likely to make the project cited.

WHAT (scope, see child beads):
1. E1: a resumable scaling sweep harness (model-size ladder ~0.5M to ~100M params, Chinchilla-style ~20x token budgets, orchestrated via the existing bench-fixed-flops subprocess pattern).
2. E2: power-law fits L(C)=a*C^(-b)+c with bootstrap CIs on exponents, THE overlay plot (log-log, all mechanisms, CI bands), and a comparative table with explicit statistical statements.
3. E3: the sequence-length crossover study testing the sub-quadratic claims (ultrametric O(N log N) vs standard O(N^2)) on wall-clock and memory from T=256 to 32k - including the honest possibility that the current torch kernel-mode port is actually quadratic with better constants.

DEPENDENCIES: requires D1 (resume - larger rungs take hours) and D2 (metrics stream feeds the fits). Verdicts feed the EP-G hypothesis ledger.

HONESTY REQUIREMENTS (bake into children): report exponent CIs even when wide; state the compute range covered and refuse extrapolation claims beyond it; both analytic FLOPs and measured wall-clock reported (relates existing bead model_guided_research-bks).

title: Scaling sweep harness (resumable model-size ladders per mechanism)
description:
WHY: scaling-law comparison (EP-E rationale) requires many runs across a model-size ladder per mechanism, each potentially hours long. Without orchestration + resumability this is operationally impossible.

WHAT: `mgr scaling-sweep --mechanism X [--ladder small|full] [--device] [--seeds N]`:
- LADDER: geometric parameter ladder, e.g. ~0.5M / 2M / 8M / 30M / 100M params. Define the exact configs in-implementation with documented rationale: fixed aspect ratio (n_embd/n_layer relationship), heads scaling with n_embd, head_dim kept compatible with mechanism constraints (%4 quaternion, %8 octonion/clifford - VALIDATE the ladder per mechanism upfront and emit a per-mechanism feasibility table).
- TOKEN BUDGETS: ~20x params (Chinchilla heuristic) at matched data; per-rung --target-flops derived and recorded; data via the standard FineWeb parquet pipeline.
- ORCHESTRATION: sequence runs via the bench-fixed-flops subprocess pattern (cli.py:1142+ precedent); per-rung artifacts under artifacts/scaling/<mechanism>/<run_id>/rung_<i>/ with summary.json + metrics.jsonl (D2); a sweep-level manifest.json tracking rung status (pending/running/done/failed) enabling RESUME of the sweep itself (skip completed rungs) on top of D1's within-run resume.
- CPU smoke mode (--ladder smoke: 2 tiny rungs) for CI-lite testing.

HONEST EXCLUSIONS: octonion at larger rungs is wall-clock-infeasible until A6 lands - the feasibility table must say so explicitly (soft-gate; do not hard-block the bead on A6). Gauge cannot do cached eval until A5 (training is fine).

RELATED: existing bead model_guided_research-2bx (config templates) - the ladder configs should BE those templates if it has landed; existing model_guided_research-wiz (dataset snapshot/split pinning) matters here: pin the data snapshot per sweep for comparability and record it in the manifest.

ACCEPTANCE:
- One command runs/sequences a full mechanism ladder, resumes after interruption at both levels (mid-rung via D1, mid-sweep via manifest);
- smoke ladder runs in CI-lite < 10 min CPU;
- manifest schema documented (consumed by E2).

DEPENDS ON: D1 (resume), D2 (metrics for fits).
notes:
POLISH ADDENDUM (tests & logging):
- LIVE PROGRESS: rich live table over rungs (rung, params, status, elapsed, current loss); sweep-end summary table with per-rung final losses and wall-clock.
- ATOMIC MANIFEST: manifest.json updates via tmp+rename (a crash mid-write must never corrupt sweep state); unit test simulates a torn write by killing between stages and asserts the manifest stays parseable and resume skips exactly the completed rungs.
- FEASIBILITY TABLE LOGGING: the per-mechanism ladder feasibility table (which rungs are valid given head_dim constraints, which are excluded and why) is logged at sweep start AND written into the manifest - exclusions must be auditable artifacts, not console ephemera.
- MAINTENANCE CONTRACT: add a smoke ladder (2 tiny rungs, 1 mechanism) stage to the rz8.8 e2e pipeline script as part of this bead's acceptance.

title: Scaling-law fits & comparative analysis report
description:
WHY: the fits are the science. A mechanism with a credibly better scaling exponent at small scale is the strongest evidence this project can produce that exotic structure matters; credibly-equal exponents with different intercepts, or worse exponents, are equally publishable negative results.

WHAT: `mgr scaling-report --runs artifacts/scaling/... [--out DIR]`, re-runnable from artifacts ALONE (no retraining):
- FITS: per mechanism, fit the saturating power law L(C) = a*C^(-b) + c (and report the plain power-law fit alongside) via robust regression on final-loss-vs-compute from the E1 manifests; use D2 metrics tails (mean of last k% steps) rather than single final values to reduce noise.
- UNCERTAINTY: bootstrap CIs on exponents b (resample seeds/rungs); state explicitly when CIs are wide - resisting over-claim IS a deliverable; refuse extrapolation statements beyond the measured compute range (hard-code the caveat into the report template).
- THE PLOT: log-log loss-vs-compute overlay, all mechanisms, CI bands (matplotlib, already a dep), plus a per-mechanism panel grid; publication-quality labeling.
- TABLE: a, b, c, fit R^2/residual diagnostics, rung count, seeds.
- STATISTICS: explicit pairwise statement on whether exponent differences are significant (bootstrap overlap test); no significance theater - if nothing is significant, the headline says so.
- VERDICTS: preregistered scaling predictions (entered via G1; e.g. "ultrametric exponent >= standard's") adjudicated and fed to the ledger (G2 when available, inline otherwise with G2-compatible JSON).

ACCEPTANCE:
- Synthetic-data unit test: generate fake rung results from a KNOWN law + noise -> fitted exponent recovers truth within the bootstrap CI (validates the whole pipeline);
- report regenerable byte-stably from fixed artifacts (deterministic bootstrap seed);
- committed report for whatever real ladders exist at execution time, with the honesty caveats rendered.

DEPENDS ON: E1 (sweep artifacts/manifest schema).

title: Sequence-length crossover study (sub-quadratic claims under measurement)
description:
WHY: the ultrametric framework's headline claim (README framework #2) is O(N log N) attention vs standard O(N^2). Nothing in the repo measures where (or whether) that asymptotic advantage materializes in the torch implementation. NOTE the honest possibility, flagged during code investigation: kernel-mode ultrametric as implemented computes ALL-PAIRS LCP match probabilities - which is O(N^2) with different constants; the genuinely sub-quadratic path is the trie decode, which is CPU-only and inference-only. If that is what measurement shows, SAYING SO is the deliverable, plus a discovered-from bead for a genuinely sub-quadratic training path.

WHAT: a benchmark (extend cli.py per-op patterns; relates existing bead model_guided_research-2cg which is fixed-size op-level - this bead is the SCALING question):
- Mechanisms: ultrametric (kernel mode; trie decode measured separately, annotated CPU/inference-only), standard SDPA, standard FlexAttention, tropical, fractal.
- Measure: wall-clock (median of repeats after warmup) and peak memory for forward AND forward+backward at T in {256, 512, 1k, 2k, 4k, 8k, 16k, 32k} (as memory permits; record OOM points - they are data), batch-adjusted to keep token counts comparable; CPU and GPU.
- ANALYSIS: log-log time-vs-T plots with fitted slopes (the empirical exponent: ~2.0 means quadratic, regardless of marketing); CROSSOVER TABLE: the T (if any) where each exotic mechanism beats standard SDPA and FlexAttention on time and on memory, per device.
- VERDICT: explicit, fed to the G1 registry: "ultrametric kernel mode empirical exponent = X; {is|is not} sub-quadratic in the tested range; crossover at T=... on <hardware> | no crossover observed".

METHODOLOGY (write into the report): warmup iterations, repeat counts, median + IQR, torch.cuda.synchronize around timings, memory via torch.cuda.max_memory_allocated / tracemalloc on CPU, single-process isolation per point (subprocess) to avoid allocator pollution.

ACCEPTANCE:
- Artifact report with plots + crossover table + fitted exponents;
- the discovered-from bead filed if the quadratic finding holds (title suggestion: "Sub-quadratic ultrametric training path (bucketed LCP attention)");
- no hard dependencies - runnable against current code.

title: Dataset/legal/licensing sanity check
description:
- Audit datasets referenced/used by nanochat scripts for licensing/attribution; ensure cached paths and download scripts respect licensing.
- Add a short note listing sources and constraints under docs/dataset_licenses.md; no code changes.

title: Dataset snapshot & split pinning for CMA-ES
description:
- Define how CMA-ES runs pin dataset versions/splits to avoid silent drift; document data paths, hashes, and split seeds used in objectives.
- Add a small helper to record dataset hash/size into artifacts per run.
acceptance_criteria: Guideline/helper for recording dataset version/split info implemented; at least one run logs dataset hash/size into artifacts/cmaes/; documented in cmaes_plan_mgr or data note.

title: Align torch/nanochat configs with bio_inspired_nanochat best practices
description:
- Compare optimizer/scheduler/compile settings (e.g., torch.compile modes, autocast/bfloat16, grad clipping, dropout, activation checkpointing) against bio_inspired_nanochat.
- Propose concrete config changes or flags to match/improve parity while preserving mathematical features.
- Capture findings in docs/config_parity.md with file/line pointers; defer code changes unless low-risk toggles.

title: CA-based initializer spike
description:
- Implement a optional cellular-automaton initializer (rule30/rule116) that generates a 1D bitfield, rescales to match fan-in/out variance (Xavier/He), and reshapes to weight tensors.
- Expose via flag/env in nanochat init utilities without touching default paths.
- Support mixing ratio (e.g., alpha*CA + (1-alpha)*standard) and deterministic seed control.
acceptance_criteria: Initializer function added and gated by flag/env; supports rule30/rule116, variance-correct scaling, optional mix alpha; no regression to default init; unit sanity check on variance; docs snippet added.
close_reason: Implemented CA initializer (rule30/rule116) with scaling, alpha mix, CLI/env wiring, variance sanity test, and README snippet.

title: Add optional torch.compile support to nanochat.train (esp for FlexAttention)
description: nanochat.train currently does not torch.compile the model, so FlexAttention warns it may run an unfused path and appears slower in fixed-FLOPs baselines. Add an opt-in --compile flag (and optionally mode/backend knobs) so baseline/bench runs can fairly compare SDPA vs Flex under compilation; record compile settings in artifacts.
acceptance_criteria: nanochat.train supports --compile (default off) on CUDA; when enabled, model forward/backward runs without errors for standard attention + --use-flex-attention; compile settings recorded in artifacts summary; tests remain green.
close_reason: Implemented torch.compile flags in nanochat.train, compile FlexAttention callable, validated via CUDA smoke runs + pytest.

title: CMA-ES for math demos (targeted knobs)
description:
- Identify 2-3 math demos where a tiny set of continuous knobs could benefit from CMA-ES (e.g., tropical sparsity/mixture lambdas, ultrametric p/packed thresholds, reversible Cayley iters, gauge structured/unstructured weights).
- Define per-demo parameter sets/bounds and propose small-budget CMA-ES sweeps; document in a note with feasibility and costs; no runs yet.
acceptance_criteria: Per-demo CMA-ES targets listed with 2-5 parameters each, bounds, cost estimates, and go/no-go notes; stored in docs/cmaes_math_targets.md; ready to schedule pilots.

title: Auto run report generator
description:
- After a harness run, generate a concise markdown (optional HTML) report summarizing config, FLOPs, seeds, metrics, math diagnostics, plots/links (vis artifacts), and pass/fail checks.
- Hook into harness pipeline; allow manual invocation on an artifacts directory.
- Ensure outputs land under artifacts/reports/ with unique names and provenance.
acceptance_criteria: Report generator produces a markdown (and optional HTML) report from a harness run; includes config, FLOPs, seeds, metrics tables, key diagnostics, links to vis; saved under artifacts/reports/; can be run automatically post-run or manually on a path.

title: Granular A/B feature benchmarking
description:
Build a harness to swap single math features (gauge transport, ultrametric routing, tropical attn, reversible coupling, FlexAttention, etc.) into an otherwise vanilla nanochat block and compare vs vanilla under identical FLOPs, batch/seq, seeds.

Collect metrics per feature:
- Train CE and Val CE (requires 5fp)
- tokens/s, TFLOP/s, memory
- Instability flags (NaN/Inf counts)

Run with multiple seeds (3-5) and compute:
- Mean ± std for each metric
- 95% CI 
- Simple significance test (Welch's t-test or similar)

Emit per-feature CSV/JSON + markdown with delta vs baseline; store under artifacts/feature_ablate/. This is THE key deliverable for proving whether exotic math features beat vanilla.
acceptance_criteria: Harness runs at least 3 features vs vanilla with matched configs; outputs structured JSON/CSV + markdown deltas; seeds logged; FLOPs budget documented; results reproducible via recorded commands.

title: Restore and enhance inline comments across nanochat core
description:
- Pass through nanochat/* (synaptic.py, gpt*.py, attention modules, optimizers, scheduler) to reintroduce explanatory comments that were removed; add brief clarifying comments where logic is dense.
- Avoid noise; ensure comments align with docs/math; no functional changes.
- Track progress by file; ensure net comment gain where applicable.
